programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jack gets closer to a conman friend who is trying to straighten out his life, but a fatal car crash complicates matters and an investigation ensues. The case leads to a luxury health spa, where Dr Sloan seizes the opportunity to operate undercover.(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A prison doctor is held hostage by one of Sloan's patients - a convicted criminal demanding to see the newscaster who covered his trial. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Perjured Parrot

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Shattered Dream
programme.desc: The owner of a large uncut diamond is charged with murder after she's swindled by a shady diamond cutter who shatters the gem, and is later found dead.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is found on the side of a remote road and toxicology tests reveal he died of overhydration. When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team search for a boy from Ralph's class who went missing during a field trip, and Toby gets closer to uncovering the identity of Happy's husband.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is called to catch thieves who have stolen a rare gem from LA's Natural History Museum, in an attempt extract materials required to enrich a nuclear bomb.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks go undercover as a married couple in a quiet suburb as part of an investigation into a Russian sleeper cell. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. Callen learns the Chameleon - a criminal out for revenge - is involved, and races to track him down. However, when he comes face to face with the fugitive, he is tricked into answering a phone wired to a car bomb.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. Callen is faced with a difficult decision when he is told to release the Chameleon in order to save Atley's life, and contemplates subduing his nemesis once and for all.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.sub-title: The Only Easy Day Is Yesterday
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: Maxi completes his tech course ahead of schedule, while a teenager with a serious spinal injury demands attention from paramedics, and a sun-worshipper in a mankini causes a dangerously wild crowd of onlookers to gather on the beach.(n)

programme.title: Medium
programme.sub-title: When Push Comes to Shove - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Allison is reunited with Captain Push, the sceptical detective she worked with in the pilot episode. The psychic agrees to help him track down a serial killer, but ends up putting his life in danger. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Anita Dobson guests as Phil Hunter's mum Lynne, who turns up at the station to lecture him on his responsibilities as a father-to-be. Smithy seizes an opportunity to meet local villain's wife Louise Larson and continue their dangerous relationship, while Adam struggles to make headway with his road safety campaign, until old friend Isaac Collins pulls a few strings at the local council to help get it under way. In court, the case against Terry's brother Ben concludes - but the outcome promises to have a devastating long-term effect on the entire family.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Gina returns to her job immediately after receiving treatment for her cancer. Clearly unfit for work but too stubborn to admit it, she relies on June to oversee the relief. Gary is shot during a raid on a gang suspected of handling stolen goods, while Phil bristles with righteous indignation at the discovery that Kate has been mugged at knifepoint and vows to catch the thug responsible. As for Adam, he hits another snag as he tries to get his road safety initiative up and running, and again asks Isaac Collins to help - unaware his old friend's shady past is about to catch up with him.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Jack embarks on a relentless search for the gunman who shot Gary - and soon realises the finger of suspicion is pointing firmly at Adam's friend Isaac Collins. Smithy and Jonathan call at Gina's house to see why she hasn't turned up for work, and discover her unconscious on the bathroom floor. Away from the line of duty, Tony feels dejected at not having a date for Honey's birthday party and decides to hire an escort for the evening - little anticipating the surprise awaiting him.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. Adam learns the Borough Commander has been sent photos of him smoking marijuana, and decides to resign before he's unceremoniously thrown off the force. Rochelle continues her steamy illicit affair with Dan, while Jack questions Neil about his relationship with Suzie Sim and receives a bland assurance that it's all professional and above board - but later sees the pair in a compromising position.(n)

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A surveillance team is sent into the restaurant to catch one of Henry's regulars in the act of bribing an MP. Drama, starring Richard Griffiths.(n)

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry cooks a banquet for a visiting Japanese delegation, and tracks down a criminal from his past, hitherto presumed dead. This shocking discovery prompts him to investigate corruption in the police force. Richard Griffiths stars.(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Dwelling Place
programme.desc: Feature-length adaptation of the best-selling novel, telling the tale of a 16-year-old struggling to keep her five brothers and sisters out of the workhouse with the help of a kindly carpenter. Starring James Fox, Tracy Whitwell and Edward Rawle-Hicks.(n)

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' story. An orphan boy endures a horrible childhood in the workhouse, but yearns for more. After being sold to a cruel undertaker, he runs away to London - where he joins a group of pickpockets. Timothy Spall heads an all-star cast as Fagin, with support from Tom Hardy, Gregor Fisher, Sarah Lancashire, Sophie Okonedo, Julian Rhind-Tutt and William Miller in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but her harsh new approach proves hard to cope with in the face of two devastating cases. A father-to-be is badly injured in an explosion, leaving him unable to work to support his family, and a couple with dwarfism expecting their first child are warned that their child may be stillborn, a situation that leaves Patsy confronted with one of the hardest decisions of her life.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Philip Henderson is found dead in his field following an altercation the previous day with his neighbour Doug Randall about his failed crop. Suspicion falls on the neighbour and his new wife, who was known to have had ongoing problems with Philip over his unpaid bar tab at her pub. However, as Mike and the team dig further into the mystery more suspects come into play. Neill Rea stars.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The search for an abducted student leads nowhere until a 'psychic adviser' to the missing girl's mother approaches the police, offering to help track her down. Burke grudgingly agrees to attend one of her shows and is stunned by the accuracy of information he is given about his father that no-one else could possibly know. Drama, starring Alex Norton and Blythe Duff.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: New eye-witness testimony and a television documentary about the McCready case casts doubt on the original murder investigation. DS Walker is suspended from duty and decides to return to Glasgow to find new evidence to prove his innocence, in order to save his career, relationship and reputation.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Snooker player Dennis Taylor asks Lovejoy for help finding a billiards table that once belonged to Mary Queen of Scots. Drama, also starring Ian McShane.(n)

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A surveillance team is sent into the restaurant to catch one of Henry's regulars in the act of bribing an MP. Drama, starring Richard Griffiths.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Those involved in Bert's death and its subsequent cover-up search for inner peace. Sarah battles her own demons, but is aware that Colin's suffering is greater. Meanwhile, James' treatment continues to cause him suffering and Jack is determined to find answers after Carolyn's revelation. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.sub-title: A Kiss to Build a Dream On
programme.desc: Elizabeth enlists Prudence's help, and on the eve of her engagement party, Sarah confides to her aunt about a shameful secret. Meanwhile, Anna and Gino's relationship moves forward and James' treatment takes a disturbing turn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack takes over as superintendent in Adam's absence, and promotes Neil Manson to acting DCI - despite grave misgivings about his recent conduct. Gabriel publicly asks Sheelagh out for dinner, allaying her fears that their relationship is over, and Ramani is devastated to learn Terry has been having an affair with his sister-in-law. Affairs of the heart also preoccupy Dan Casper, who receives another poison pen letter - but decides to ignore it and continue his affair with Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Helena develops complications following Nick's rushed surgery, putting Diane in a difficult position when she realises the problem should have been noticed during the first operation. Sam breaks the rules to help a recovering alcoholic who needs a heart transplant, and Justin tries to flirt with Kyla again. Jesse Birdsall guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A bogus paramedic causes chaos among the staff and patients of Holby City, and a mugging incident results in Adam having to reveal a secret. Medical drama, starring Ian Bleasdale and Cathy Shipton.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Jack is forced to arrest his own son Benjamin for possession of drugs, and the corpulent form of convicted rapist Peter Baxter again crosses Sam's path as she helps Ramani investigate a nurse's disappearance. Gabriel invites Sheelagh back to his place in a bid to prove that he has finally managed to forget about Kerry Young, while Yvonne is summoned to hear her fate as the inquiry into the death of Lee Chapman concludes.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Arthur reflect on their marriage and parenting skills when their son Martin is suddenly taken to hospital. Pat moves in with Nick and her grandson much to Nigels dismay.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frank and the Mitchell brothers take Sharon, Kathy and Pat to France on a booze cruise, will romance bloom for the couples or will it all end in classic EastEnders fashion.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Billy is rescued from a tank of caustic soda and rushed to hospital in agony while Nick returns to work just as a bombing campaign presents the team with a fresh challenge.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Lexie's plans to organise a clan gathering, which include hiring a film crew, look set to be thwarted when pernickety Charles MacDonald throws a spanner in the works. Duncan and Jess struggle to admit their feelings for each other and Molly prepares to make a confession. Scottish drama, starring Dawn Steele and Susan Hampshire.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: A humiliated and jealous Evelyn exacts revenge by spreading the sordid secrets of Karen's past around Skelthwaite and patients soon refuse to be treated by her, leaving it up to Anna to discover why a teenage epileptic continues to suffer attacks despite his medication. Simon struggles to cope with the preparations for his forthcoming wedding and proves easy prey for Evelyn, who seems intent on wreaking as much havoc as she can.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sisters Sharon and Tracey's lives are changed for ever when their husbands leave for work and never return. First episode of the popular comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie puts Marlene under pressure to fit in with the locals, so she decides to take up cookery - an idea that does not go down well with her family. She orders her husband to pick blackberries for a pie, causing him to inadvertently trespass on the land of his anti-English neighbour. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean falls out with Lionel, but the tense atmosphere between them does not prevent a visit from her sister-in-law Penny and husband Stephen. Bob Larbey's comedy about late-blossoming love, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy accepts Sid's challenge to play a round of golf - despite not having touched a club since 1939. Brian Wilde and Sid Comer star.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The duo are called out to a private zoo, where human remains have been found in the tigers' den. The evidence points to an unfortunate accident, but Dalziel suspects foul play - and a connection to a mysterious disappearance he investigated 20 years previously. Detective drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the killing of notorious biker-gang leader Eddie Chapman when his son's girlfriend brings them new information about his death. She reveals her partner must avenge his father's murder as part of a final initiation into the gang, but believes the wrong man is being targeted. Meanwhile, Sandra is forced to reassess her position at Ucos when a former colleague suggests she is wasting her career. Starring Amanda Redman, with her real-life daughter Emily Glenister in a supporting role.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd is unsettled when Sarah Cavendish (played by Five Daughters star Eva Birthistle), an officer of equal rank to him, joins the team to investigate the disappearance of a banker who had been struggling to cope with losing his daughter to cancer. Though Cavendish's professional skills quickly prove to be a major asset, her new colleagues discover that a failed operation from earlier in her career is continuing to haunt her, and become concerned for her welfare. Trevor Eve stars, with Sue Johnston and Tara FitzGerald.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part One

programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret.(n)

programme.desc: Sarah reveals her horrific secret to George, and their relationship is suddenly thrown into doubt. The Christmas soiree brings out Jack's intimacy issues, and Anna publicly humiliates Andrew. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.desc: Helena's post-mortem reveals the error made during Nick's surgery, but Diane realises unless the surgeon comes clean, she could be the one to take the blame. Joseph's condition grows noticeably worse, and a prison inmate is admitted to the hospital after being injured in a riot. Former EastEnders actor Todd Carty guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sean is devastated to discover the truth about Max and Tina, and Adam feels pressured to reveal his secret. Meanwhile, Patrick is amazed when a blind man inexplicably regains his sight - could it be a miracle? Medical drama, starring Gerald Kyd, Pal Aron and Ian Kelsey.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Peter Baxter is released on bail, even though there is evidence linking him to the disappearance of nurse Manisha Devi - but the subsequent discovery of her burnt body raises the possibility that a dreadful mistake has been made. Samantha presses ahead with the investigation, and identifies another suspect in the form of taxi driver David Russo. Unfortunately, she isn't aware that Ramani is already in the back of his cab, being driven through the streets of Sun Hill by someone who may be a killer.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon and Grant are on a romantic weekend and Phil and Kathy are getting to know each other better. Meanwhile Frank and Pat are visiting Diane who is heavily pregnant.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard goes green with jealousy when he sees Phil and Kathy together, Nigel confronts Nick as he tries to steal his mothers furniture and Arthur comes clean about his double life.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Angry protestors plant a fire-bomb in a chemist's shop, unaware the manager is still on the premises. Billy finally meets his mother, and Hallam fends off lovelorn student Jenny.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Lexie is annoyed when her estranged father arrives in Glenbogle, but he soon sets about charming the rest of the MacDonald clan with his tall stories. Molly and Golly pose as a married couple to buy a horse, but are forced to keep up the pretence for longer than they anticipated, and Duncan's plans to impress Jess go awry when it becomes clear they have very different tastes in interior decoration.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The locals bid an emotional farewell to Vic and Lucy, and Simon's problems continue to mount as his business crumbles and he is consumed by guilt over his fling with Evelyn. Elsewhere, a dishy gardener steals the heart of a wheelchair-bound patient, while Tom and Beth take advantage of a drunken Joe's incapacity and steal a kiss. Rural drama, starring Thomas Craig and Tony Haygarth.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene allows Boycie to buy a maximum of six cows for the farm - and is less than impressed when Elgin, Jed and Bryan turn up with a herd of 300. The vet is called out when Rocky the bull fails to take any notice of the females, and Tyler prepares to sit a local private school's entrance exam. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel is concerned that he has no pension plans, so the ever-resourceful Alistair comes up with an ingenious idea - to sell the country house. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo's nephew is getting married, and while the groom's uncle considers having a bath to mark the occasion, Foggy fears disaster will befall the happy couple.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The judge presides over the case of a man who killed a paedophile, an act that Jo defends by arguing it protected further victims from abuse. Meanwhile, she meets the real father of the boy she is trying to adopt, and begins seeing him socially - sending Deed into a fit of jealous rage. Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the murder of a man found in an alleyway seven years previously when he is identified on a website covering unexplained disappearances. The victim is named by a woman whose husband hired him six months before his death, but neither of them had thought anything of it when their employee went missing - and seem to know little about him. Meanwhile, Brian and Gerry are distracted by looming job cuts and fear that Ucos could be downsized. With Robert Daws and Claudie Blakley.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman who went missing from a care home in 1986 is found murdered in a burnt-out car, and the team discovers she had recently abducted a teenage girl herself. As the investigation continues, both cases appear to be linked to an urban myth about a character known as the Bag Man - an eyeless villain who kidnaps children. With Eva Birthistle, Tara FitzGerald and Wil Johnson.(n)

programme.title: The 1981 London Marathon: A Historical First
programme.desc: In March 1981, the very first London marathon was held, with just over 7,000 entrants taking part in the race. while nearly 40 years on there are now more than 40,000 participants, making it one of the biggest and best-loved events in the sporting calendar. This special programme looks back at that historic first staging of the event, reliving the remarkable finish to the men's race - which ended in a dead heat - and all observed through the backdrop of how London looked then.(n)

programme.title: Penguins - Spy in the Huddle
programme.desc: David Tennant narrates an insight into this most intriguing and comical of birds, beginning with three different species' journeys to reach their breeding grounds. Emperor penguins cross a treacherous frozen sea, and rockhoppers brave stormy seas, before they come ashore and face a daunting assault up a 300ft cliff where they are vulnerable to predators. Meanwhile, the tropical humboldts have to evade sea lions, dodge vampire bats and battle sea birds in order to lay their eggs in the desert. All the while, their every move is being secretly filmed by hidden spy technology, including robotic penguins with cameras for eyes.(n)

programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson travel to Shefford in Bedfordshire, where they meet a couple who claim their garden is basic and plain. Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A 22-year-old father-to-be must face his fear of surgery when his partner needs a caesarean section after the baby's heart rate starts to dip. A mother of four boys prepares to give birth to her fifth child, and being an old hand at delivery, soon has a new addition to her brood with no complications. A high-risk pregnancy is closely monitored, and a woman who has been in the hospital for a week is still waiting for the birth of her first baby to begin - but she and her partner are prepared to work as a team when the time comes.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie returns to active duty in Afghanistan in the run-up to election day, and discovers that the fake Omar being held in custody has admitted to being a decoy. Georgie is advised to keep a low profile following her recent troubles, but after an assassination attempt on a US dignitary, she realises what is really going on - Elvis' killer is closer to home than she thought and planning a devastating attack. Mimi comes to terms with her past, Throbber earns his stripes, and Dr Bahil looks to the future.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter F. The Hairy Bikers cook a focaccia and Nigel Slater serves roast figs with honey and marsala.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke turn their attention to a bland, rented front-space in Dalkeith, Midlothian, which they hope to transform into a productive and pretty cottage garden.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The green-fingered team guides team enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. Monty Don and faithful hounds Nigel and Nellie are at Longmeadow to bring inspiration as well as outline all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Co-presenter Rachel de Thame visits stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the first edition of a new look for the programme, Anita Rani is on her home patch in east London to discover what lockdown means for Woodberry Wetlands and what the future holds for the animals of Hackney City Farm. At home, she gets stuck into reinvigorating her garden and cooks up a barbecue tandoori. Adam Henson reveals the effects of the pandemic on farming and Tom Heap investigates how a new British land army might work for fruit and veg growers.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson is given unique access to the Royal Botanic Gardens in Edinburgh and George allows us unique access to his precious tool shed. Plus, Carole Baxter and Brian Cunningham answer viewers' gardening questions.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring South West chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by comedian and children's author Nina Wadia, who has a special interest in cuisine for children with diabetes. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp (1943)
programme.desc: A Home Guard officer serving during the Second World War is outraged by the disrespect for tradition shown by younger soldiers. He looks back on his military adventures during the Boer War and First World War, as well as his three great loves. Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger's satirical drama, starring Roger Livesey, Deborah Kerr, Anton Walbrook and John Laurie.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Stephen Hendry v Ronnie O'Sullivan. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the semi-final at the 2002 World Championship, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison come up with virtual reality designs to transform a Halifax couple's 19th-century home. When owner Paula developed MS, it became vital to make the house more wheelchair accessible - but her partner Jeremy also sees an opportunity to give it a stylish new look. Laura's concept makes the most of the views of the garden and uses clever recycling tricks to stretch the budget, while Robert deploys off-the-shelf materials to making the place more accessible.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a soup factory in Wigan that produces 2.5 million cans a day, and follows the production of a tin of vegetable soup from a pea harvest to the eventual dispatch. He also encounters a pressure cooker that stands 27.4 metres tall. Cherry Healey compares the vitamin content of fresh and frozen vegetables, and visits a salt mine in Crewe, while historian Ruth Goodman cooks up a butterted spinach soup using a recipe from a 17th-century cookbook, which is the earliest known reference to soup in English.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. In the first episode, Simon visits Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams embark on the final leg of their journey, heading off on the 4,800 kilometre trek to the finish line in Ushuaia, Argentina, the most southerly city in the world. However, as they prepare to leave the last checkpoint, one of the teams reveals that they have run out of money and will not be completing the challenge. The three remaining pairs rely on different tactics to get ahead, benefiting from risky gambles, lucky opportunities and the kindness of strangers.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter reports on how southern Spain has become the main gateway for drugs into Europe, leading to violent turf wars between gangs and a government crackdown as the police struggle to regain control of their streets. Stacey goes on aerial patrol with police officers guarding the strip of water between Morocco and Spain, and discovers the extraordinary lengths smugglers will take to evade them, including travelling at 100mph in speedboats during extreme weather conditions.(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Gemma, Ray and Kieran's unity is challenged by a sudden tragedy, and a misunderstanding also leads to a showdown. Gemma rushes back to her childhood home in the wake of the shocking turn of events, Kieran suffers a panic attack which calls his future as a paramedic into question, and Ray finds herself faced with a hard decision.(n)

programme.title: Limmy's Homemade Show
programme.desc: Rough-and-ready self-made comedy show featuring Scottish comedian Limmy, aka Brian Limond. Limmy feels guilty about accidentally leaving his phone's stopwatch running and has a lifesaving idea that Scotland can give to the world. He is also unhappy about the placement of the letter Q in the alphabet.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The fragile truce between the vampires and Staten Island's werewolves is put to the test, and only a trial by combat can resolve matters. Colin Robinson finds romance with a new co-worker, who can match him for her ability to spread misery. Arj Barker (Flight of the Conchords) guest stars as the leader of the gang's lycanthropic rivals.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The housemates venture into Manhattan to try and form an alliance with a powerful old friend, who has risen to become the king of the city's vampires. Laszlo dons his most sinister hat for the occasion, despite Nadja's protests that it is cursed, while Guillermo is left feeling unappreciated by his master.(n)

programme.title: Titian - Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The story behind the artist's commission for Philip II of Spain, known as the poesie paintings, which had not been seen together for over 450 years and had been the subject of an exhibition at London's National Gallery before it was closed due to the Covid-19 outbreak. Cameras were recently granted access to bring this groundbreaking exhibition to the screens across Britain and beyond.(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists seek a serial scam artist faking disability to claim thousands of pounds in insurance payouts, and also uncover the most prolific dental fraudster ever. Plus, a preacher turns prisoner when he's caught with his hand in the public purse. Presented by Michelle Ackerley.(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A couple whose car was hit by a truck on the M1 while on a journey to celebrate their wedding anniversary. Plus, a wing suit free-fall enthusiast who hit trouble when his main parachute became tangled up, 30 seconds from the ground. Presented by Nick Knowles.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: New products include a unisex t-shirt that repels water-based liquids and kills odour-causing bacteria and a Champagne bottle opener. Presented by Lucy Alexander.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin with a spectacular challenge as the nine remaining contestants head to the Royal Navy base in Portsmouth, where they have to cook for HMS Diamond's Trafalgar Night celebration dinner. Split into three teams, they face the enormous challenge of mass catering at fine-dining level from the ship's galley. They then return to the MasterChef kitchen, where they must create a dish to hold onto their hard-fought place in the competition, with two being sent home at the end by judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Westminster, presented by Alicia McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: City in the Sky
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell and Hannah Fry present a documentary exploring aviation, beginning with a look at the challenges of getting aircraft into the air. The programme features a visit to the world's busiest airport to discover the work that goes into getting its millions of visitors off the ground. Plus, the construction of the world's largest passenger plane and the dangers of taking off from the coldest city on Earth.(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Adam Partridge search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Britain's longest-running industrial strike.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Beattie Edmondson, Amol Rajan, Gaby Roslin and Hugh Dennis test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma prepare for their first-ever cattle show, but before that they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show.(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Iolo explores the Brecon Beacons during summer and autumn, witnessing sheep being gathered by shepherds on horseback in the Black Mountain foothills and dedicated volunteers try to repair the mountaintop. In the Usk Valley, bats feed before they hibernate in caves and migrating ducks on Talybont Reservoir ready for winter.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the sixth and concluding semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of Teotihuacan, Derry Girls, the Silk Road, and Olympic fencing, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Ken Dodd: How Tickled We Were
programme.desc: A celebration of the life and work of the comedian, recounting his life from his boyhood growing up in the 1930s in Knotty Ash through to his big break and continued career in showbusiness. Dodd's career spanned 50 years and after his rapid rise to stardom, he maintained his presence via countless television and radio shows, theatre tours and summer seasons with work ranging from pantomime to Shakespeare. He also enjoyed a spell as a successful recording artist and remained active until just a few months before his death aged 90. This programme features contributions from his friends and colleagues and includes an interview with his widow Anne.(n)

programme.title: Blackpool: Big Night Out
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the Lancashire seaside resort through the eyes of acts who have performed there. Blackpool was once at the heart of the British entertainment industry and launched the careers of Morecambe and Wise, as well as attracting world-famous artists including Frank Sinatra. With contributions by Ken Dodd, Roy 'Chubby' Brown, Syd Little and Cannon and Ball.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the first edition of a new look for the programme, Anita Rani is on her home patch in east London to discover what lockdown means for Woodberry Wetlands and what the future holds for the animals of Hackney City Farm. At home, she gets stuck into reinvigorating her garden and cooks up a barbecue tandoori. Adam Henson reveals the effects of the pandemic on farming and Tom Heap investigates how a new British land army might work for fruit and veg growers.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. In the first episode, Simon visits Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A doctor and a paramedic operate on a mountainside to save a hiker who fell 650ft, while a motorist takes evasive action when a car heads towards him on the wrong side of the road.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: More products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on a gadget for wheelie bins, a measuring tool and dry shampoo for children.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival is Suzanne from London, who hopes that mechanical expert Steve Fletcher can bring a decorative wind-up bird cage that once belonged to her late mother back to its former glory. Meanwhile, church wardens Brian Tidswell and David Dickinson bring in a selection of carved angels for wood restorer Will Kirk to repair. Elsewhere, an old Georgian peat bucket and a surgeon's leather case are restored by experts Dominic Chinea and Suzie Fletcher.(n)

programme.title: City in the Sky
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell and Dr Hannah Fry examine the advanced technology needed to ensure the safety of the one million people estimated to be travelling by plane at any given time. They discover the daily routines of air traffic controllers in the busiest airspace in the world and pilots flying thousands of miles at night. Plus, transporting horses by planes and in-flight medical advice.(n)

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: MasterChef meets The Apprentice as Alex Polizzi takes on the challenge of finding new head chefs for restaurants that have a desperate need to fill a vacancy. Over four nights, she offers up a total of nine candidates who are put through a rigorous interview and testing process, with only the very best making it through to the final. She begins her search at The Gilpin in the Lake District, a fine dining establishment whose family owners want to find a new executive head chef who will care as much about the business as they do.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Ken Doherty v Paul Hunter. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the semi-final at the 2003 World Championship, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Natasha Raskin Sharp are at Culzean Castle in Ayrshire to assess items including a toy car from Germany and an art deco Carlton Ware vase. Paul Martin presents.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Beattie Edmondson, Amol Rajan, Gaby Roslin and Hugh Dennis test their general knowledge in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, with a winner declared each Friday.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Representatives of two inner city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show to raise awareness and raise funds for their important community work.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams embark on the final leg of their journey, heading off on the 4,800 kilometre trek to the finish line in Ushuaia, Argentina, the most southerly city in the world. However, as they prepare to leave the last checkpoint, one of the teams reveals that they have run out of money and will not be completing the challenge. The three remaining pairs rely on different tactics to get ahead, benefiting from risky gambles, lucky opportunities and the kindness of strangers.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality redesigns for a spacious Wiltshire home that used to be a hair salon - so is poorly designed for actually living in, with a ground floor that feels like two separate properties, each with its own front door. Laura gives the building a welcoming entrance while making the interior family-friendly and functional, while Robert comes up with an innovative approach that cuts down on the amount of floor space but which could radically alter the way the owners live.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a liqueur factory in Ireland that produces 540,000 bottles a day. He tracks the journey of a bottle of cream liqueur through the three-year production timeline. At the other end of the journey, Cherry Healey discovers where the empty bottles are recycled. Plus, historian Ruth Goodman visits a monastery to discover the spiritual origins of liqueurs.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes, Stacey Solomon, Joe Sugg, Alice Levine and Phil Wang go out for dinner together. They play a series of games, with the loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Hubble: The Wonders of Space Revealed
programme.desc: Since its launch in April 1990, the Hubble space telescope has made more than 1.5 million observations of the universe. One of the most productive scientific instruments in history, over the past three decades it has transformed man's view of the cosmos. This documentary tells the craft's remarkable story, drawing upon contributions by the astronauts who spacewalked on Hubble to carry out critical repairs and upgrades and the astronomers who have used its unique powers to make new discoveries about the universe.(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Cromford in Derbyshire, which was at the heart of 18th-century industrialist Richard Arkwright's mechanised cotton mills and a textile revolution. Ben discovers Cromford became a new kind of village, built to service the enterprise of this powerful man.(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Exploring the mountains of Dhofar in Oman, where the annual Indian Ocean monsoon has helped create a lost world of waterfalls and cloud forests filled with fascinating wildlife. The programme shows green sea turtles coming ashore in their thousands, shadowed by egg-stealing foxes, as well as footage of striped hyenas doing battle with Arabian wolves. Plus, a glimpse into the lives of chameleons, honey badgers and the elusive Arabian leopard.(n)

programme.title: King Lear (2018)
programme.desc: Richard Eyre's adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy, starring Anthony Hopkins as the ageing monarch who decides to divide his kingdom between his three daughters, only to turn against the youngest and banish her when she refuses to flatter his ego. The remaining sisters are corrupted by their newfound power and drive him into the wastelands, at a terrible cost to his sanity, while the land is plunged into chaos and civil war. With Emma Thompson, Emily Watson, Jim Broadbent, Andrew Scott and Florence Pugh.(n)

programme.title: Get Animated! BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Film critic Ali Plumb celebrates emerging animators making fresh short films, which feature malicious toasters, cheeky Glaswegian pigeons, job-haunting ghosts and smelly fungi.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of British Animation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring animation in the UK from the Victorian era to the present day, taking in the work of Bob Godfrey, John Halas & Joy Batchelor, Len Lye and Aardman Animations, and examining the ingenious methods that low-budget British productions have used to create their own distinctive styles.(n)

programme.title: The Brontes at the BBC
programme.desc: A look back at how the Bronte sisters' novels have been portrayed on TV over the last 60 years, as each generation of writers and directors has put their spin on the characters. The heroines of the novels have been portrayed in various ways, from 1950s housewives to empowered modern women, while their male counterparts have appeared as prigs, wife beaters and brooding rock stars.(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.sub-title: Build It And They Will Come
programme.desc: Professor Richard Clay asks whether utopian visions for living can ever reconcile the tension between the group and the individual, the rules and the desire to break free. In America he encounters experimental communities searching for greater meaning in life. Interviewees include architect Norman Foster and designer Shoji Sadao.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Exploring the mountains of Dhofar in Oman, where the annual Indian Ocean monsoon has helped create a lost world of waterfalls and cloud forests filled with fascinating wildlife. The programme shows green sea turtles coming ashore in their thousands, shadowed by egg-stealing foxes, as well as footage of striped hyenas doing battle with Arabian wolves. Plus, a glimpse into the lives of chameleons, honey badgers and the elusive Arabian leopard.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he paints a scene depicting a cloudy sky above a turbulent seascape of crashing waves.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Exploring national collections at a time of enforced closure due to the Covid-19 pandemic, beginning with Alastair Sooke examining Tate Modern's Andy Warhol exhibition. Covering works from across the American artist's career, he explores Warhol's long-running commitment to experimental film and TV, as well as his fascination with advertising, pop music and commerce.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Romans with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The professor continues her exploration of the real ancient Rome, descending into the streets to reveal a vast metropolis with little urban planning and poor sanitation. Mary also finds out where the Romans went to hang out, get drunk, have sex and get clean, and visits the Forum, where all manner of activities took place, from gambling and prostitution to dentistry. Along the way she tells the story of three citizens, including an apartment dweller who lived in fear of the rent collector and a seven-year-old girl killed in one of the city's many fires.(n)

programme.title: The Price of Everything (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the labyrinthine art world of the 21st century and examines both the place of art and artistic passion in a money-driven, consumer-based society. Featuring collectors, dealers, auctioneers and artists including Jeff Koons, Gerhard Richter and Njideka Akunyili Crosby and Larry Poons, the film exposes deep contradictions as it holds a mirror up to the values of the modern era, coaxing out the dynamics at play in pricing the priceless.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the golden age of piracy during the early 18th century, when Captain Kidd, Blackbeard, Calico Jack and others were in operation on the high seas. He charts the devastating impact they had during an era of colonial expansion and how they became the most wanted outlaws in the world by plundering the vast network of maritime trade, they became the most wanted outlaws in the world.(n)

programme.title: Return to Larkinland
programme.desc: AN Wilson presents a tribute to Philip Larkin, in which he recalls his own encounters with the poet in his later years. The programme traces Larkin's childhood in Coventry and studies at Oxford, and also examines how remarks made in his selected letters and biography which marked his posthumous reputation with allegations of bigotry.(n)

programme.title: Through the Lens of Larkin
programme.desc: John Wedgwood Clarke looks through more than 5,000 personal photographs taken by Philip Larkin that were found after his death and asks what they say about his work.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he paints a scene depicting a cloudy sky above a turbulent seascape of crashing waves.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Exploring national collections at a time of enforced closure due to the Covid-19 pandemic, beginning with Alastair Sooke examining Tate Modern's Andy Warhol exhibition. Covering works from across the American artist's career, he explores Warhol's long-running commitment to experimental film and TV, as well as his fascination with advertising, pop music and commerce.(n)

programme.title: The Price of Everything (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the labyrinthine art world of the 21st century and examines both the place of art and artistic passion in a money-driven, consumer-based society. Featuring collectors, dealers, auctioneers and artists including Jeff Koons, Gerhard Richter and Njideka Akunyili Crosby and Larry Poons, the film exposes deep contradictions as it holds a mirror up to the values of the modern era, coaxing out the dynamics at play in pricing the priceless.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a frozen pond surrounded by snow drifts, warmed by a rich sky and late autumn foliage.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Simon Schama presents a virtual tour of the Young Rembrandt exhibition at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, which features works from the first 10 years of the Dutch master's career. He reflects on how Rembrandt's work is particularly relevant to the current crisis, with its portrayal of the fragile nature of human happiness.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox.(n)

programme.title: Expedition Volcano
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team takes a helicopter to the crater of an active volcano in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has rarely been studied. The mountain is surrounded by forests, which provide cover for armed militant groups, and the scientists will only have a few short hours to gather information. They then move on to the surrounding area, a dangerous landscape marked by craters from past eruptions.(n)

programme.title: Bob Monkhouse: The Last Stand
programme.desc: Cameras capture the quiz show host and comedian in the summer of 2003 - just months before his death - as he entertains a room full of fellow comics with stand-up, chat and a masterclass in his craft. The night became the stuff of legend among comedians, but was never transmitted in full until recently.(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: Angela Thorne narrates a look back at clips of the comedian and presenter's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows, which have become classic viewing.(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak returns to Italy to trace the Renaissance from its perceived origins with Giotto and takes a look at the importance of religious narrative in Italian art. He reveals that the majority of Italian painting and sculpture in the 14th and 15th centuries was created to inspire devotion, especially in the work of Piero della Francesca, Michelangelo, Boticelli, Giotto, Leonardo, Raphael and Fra Angelico.(n)

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?(n)

programme.title: The 1981 London Marathon: A Historical First
programme.desc: In March 1981, the very first London marathon was held, with just over 7,000 entrants taking part in the race. while nearly 40 years on there are now more than 40,000 participants, making it one of the biggest and best-loved events in the sporting calendar. This special programme looks back at that historic first staging of the event, reliving the remarkable finish to the men's race - which ended in a dead heat - and all observed through the backdrop of how London looked then.(n)

On the way he goes fossil hunting, searches for an elusive chough and learns the story of a pregnancy-testing frog. He begins his walk at St Donat's Castle, once owned by newspaper magnate Sir Randolph Hearst, which became a party palace for the rich and famous, then heads for Dunraven Bay.(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents an appeal on behalf of Whizz-Kidz, a charity working to transform the lives of disabled children across the UK, supporting them to become confident and independent young adults.(n)

programme.title: The 1981 London Marathon: A Historical First
programme.desc: In March 1981, the very first London marathon was held, with just over 7,000 entrants taking part in the race. while nearly 40 years on there are now more than 40,000 participants, making it one of the biggest and best-loved events in the sporting calendar. This special programme looks back at that historic first staging of the event, reliving the remarkable finish to the men's race - which ended in a dead heat - and all observed through the backdrop of how London looked then.(n)

programme.title: Penguins - Spy in the Huddle
programme.desc: David Tennant narrates an insight into this most intriguing and comical of birds, beginning with three different species' journeys to reach their breeding grounds. Emperor penguins cross a treacherous frozen sea, and rockhoppers brave stormy seas, before they come ashore and face a daunting assault up a 300ft cliff where they are vulnerable to predators. Meanwhile, the tropical humboldts have to evade sea lions, dodge vampire bats and battle sea birds in order to lay their eggs in the desert. All the while, their every move is being secretly filmed by hidden spy technology, including robotic penguins with cameras for eyes.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson travel to Shefford in Bedfordshire, where they meet a couple who claim their garden is basic and plain. Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

Items assessed by the team include two sculptures made out of ostrich eggs and a watch designed for astronauts to wear on the Moon, while military expert Robert Tilney unearths some top secret D-Day plans.(n)

programme.desc: Gray and Sonia are puzzled by Whitney's disappearance, Linda feels uncomfortable when she spots Phil at her alcoholics support group and Suki questions Chantelle's dedication to her new job - but luckily Kheerat intervenes. programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: In a school in a small town in the west of Ireland, Connell is a well-liked, good-looking and athletic student, while Marianne is a proud, intimidating and unpopular loner who actively avoids her classmates and questions her teachers' authority. An attraction sparks between the two when Connell comes to collect his mother Lorraine, who works at Marianne's house, and a deep connection grows between the two teenagers - one they cannot stop but that they are determined to conceal from their peers. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: The teenagers' relationship continues in secret and they sleep together for the first time. programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Has the Government Failed the NHS - Panorama
programme.desc: Doctors and nurses have been warning for weeks that they don't have enough protective kit to stay safe. Richard Bilton investigates whether the government let down health workers leading the fight against the coronavirus, looking at the delays and mistakes that may have put the lives of NHS staff at risk.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Sonia are puzzled by Whitney's disappearance, Linda feels uncomfortable when she spots Phil at her alcoholics support group and Suki questions Chantelle's dedication to her new job - but luckily Kheerat intervenes. Patrick and Isaac start to bond although Isaac is left suspicious when Patrick drops a hint about the truth. Dotty promotes a club night at Ruby's in an attempt to make some much-needed cash - and takes the opportunity to flirt with Peter as she invites him along. Callum nervously waits for the results of his police assessment and Mitch quizzes Mick about the future of the Vic.(n)

programme.title: Dancing Back in Time
programme.desc: Carrie Grant and Tura Arutura have just two weeks to teach residents of Strabane how to swing dance for a performance at a show band spectacular. The town was once home to the famous Clipper Carlton showband, which broke new ground for the way dance hall music was performed.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: In a school in a small town in the west of Ireland, Connell is a well-liked, good-looking and athletic student, while Marianne is a proud, intimidating and unpopular loner who actively avoids her classmates and questions her teachers' authority. An attraction sparks between the two when Connell comes to collect his mother Lorraine, who works at Marianne's house, and a deep connection grows between the two teenagers - one they cannot stop but that they are determined to conceal from their peers. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: The teenagers' relationship continues in secret and they sleep together for the first time. At school, Connell makes excuses for why he didn't see his friends at the weekend and evades questions about his mother's job, while Marianne must find a way to explain her unplanned absences to her prying brother Alan.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Peter realises Bobby has a crush on Dotty and knocks his brother's confidence - but it doesn't stop the teenager turning up at the club and doing his best to impress the hostess. Linda is adamant she cannot return to her support group with Phil there, leaving Shirley and Mick frustrated. A powercut adds to the tensions between Gray and Chantelle, while Denise realises Patrick and Sheree are both hiding something and hatches a plan to uncover what is going on.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A 22-year-old father-to-be must face his fear of surgery when his partner needs a caesarean section after the baby's heart rate starts to dip. A mother of four boys prepares to give birth to her fifth child, and being an old hand at delivery, soon has a new addition to her brood with no complications. A high-risk pregnancy is closely monitored, and a woman who has been in the hospital for a week is still waiting for the birth of her first baby to begin - but she and her partner are prepared to work as a team when the time comes.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie returns to active duty in Afghanistan in the run-up to election day, and discovers that the fake Omar being held in custody has admitted to being a decoy. Georgie is advised to keep a low profile following her recent troubles, but after an assassination attempt on a US dignitary, she realises what is really going on - Elvis' killer is closer to home than she thought and planning a devastating attack. Mimi comes to terms with her past, Throbber earns his stripes, and Dr Bahil looks to the future.(n)

This time though she has a secret weapon and is determined to make it 13th time lucky. Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Lockdown: Diaries from the Frontline
programme.desc: Documentary featuring a wide cross-section of people as they record their own stories on camera phones - charting the everyday courage, kindness, anxiety and humour they experience as the nation strives to 'Keep Calm and Carry On'. From shopkeepers to home-schooling parents, care workers to cleaners, teachers to nurses, the film reveals what HM The Queen recently called 'our quiet, good humoured resolve'. Others - such as expectant Mums and the sick and elderly - show their bravery in the face of the coronavirus.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: For the final leg of their US jaunt, the trio arrive in Texas, a meat-lover's paradise where Gordon Ramsay is keen to introduce his companions to some of the best barbecue food known to man. In Lexington, Gordon turns lumberjack in an attempt to fell a dead oak - which is used to infuse ovens with wood smoke. There's a break for a bit of skinny dipping, before the trio fish go fishing, and stay in a strange cabin deep in the woods, surviving on beans and bourbon. They then hit the state capital of Austin, dressing as cowboys in the local bar and at the rodeo, before one last barbecue supper of slow-smoked beef brisket, pork ribs and spicy sausage.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott continues his tour of the Moroccan city of Marrakesh, beginning with a pottery lesson from a man who has been making pots for more than 50 years. He also hits the souks in search of local pastries, reveals the secret of a perfect cup of mint tea and learns how to prepare a classic tanjia, which is taken to be cooked in the fire of a neighbourhood hammam. Along the way he cooks harissa and preserved lemon chicken skewers, and tagine.(n)

programme.desc: A fertility expert pioneers a treatment enabling men to become pregnant and carry babies to full term. programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star.(n)

programme.title: Our Queen at War
programme.desc: How the life of the young Princess Elizabeth was shaped by the events of the Second World War, and the extraordinary experiences she went through during her teenage years. The film draws on archive material and interviews with people who shared her experiences, and royal experts explore how she was transformed from a shy princess into Queen Elizabeth II and Britain's longest reigning monarch.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Grandmother Wendy is confident of passing but she is barely in the driving seat before nerves take hold. Seventeen-year-old boy racer Callum reckons he is more than test-ready, although his mum is not so sure, and Hull woman Shaunna returns after her failure in episode one. This time though she has a secret weapon and is determined to make it 13th time lucky. Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Lockdown: Diaries from the Frontline
programme.desc: Documentary featuring a wide cross-section of people as they record their own stories on camera phones - charting the everyday courage, kindness, anxiety and humour they experience as the nation strives to 'Keep Calm and Carry On'. From shopkeepers to home-schooling parents, care workers to cleaners, teachers to nurses, the film reveals what HM The Queen recently called 'our quiet, good humoured resolve'. Others - such as expectant Mums and the sick and elderly - show their bravery in the face of the coronavirus.(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts a spin-off from The Chase in which contestants take on two, three, four or all five of the quiz's resident experts Mark Labbett, Anne Hegerty, Paul Sinha, Jenny Ryan and Shaun Wallace at once. programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: For the final leg of their US jaunt, the trio arrive in Texas, a meat-lover's paradise where Gordon Ramsay is keen to introduce his companions to some of the best barbecue food known to man. In Lexington, Gordon turns lumberjack in an attempt to fell a dead oak - which is used to infuse ovens with wood smoke. There's a break for a bit of skinny dipping, before the trio fish go fishing, and stay in a strange cabin deep in the woods, surviving on beans and bourbon. They then hit the state capital of Austin, dressing as cowboys in the local bar and at the rodeo, before one last barbecue supper of slow-smoked beef brisket, pork ribs and spicy sausage.(n)

programme.desc: In the aftermath of a massive earthquake in California, a Los Angeles rescue-chopper pilot makes a dangerous journey with his ex-wife across the state to try to rescue his daughter. Disaster movie, starring Dwayne Johnson and Carla Gugino.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is the new coach of Quahog's minor league baseball team and accidentally becomes the face of social justice when he kneels during the national anthem after overeating.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After the local airline pilots go on strike, Quagmire looks for new work and becomes a gigolo. When his clients begin to avoid paying for his services, he asks Peter for help getting his money. Meanwhile, Brian is kicked off Peter's health insurance, so he gets a job at a hardware store, until Stewie gets him fired and things 'get medical'.(n)

programme.title: Home Alone with Joel Dommett
programme.desc: The comedian presents an entertainment show from his home. Featuring celebrity call-ins, comedy interviews, and games with self-isolated viewers around the country, and providing a glimpse into Joel's everyday life.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: James Woods takes viewers behind the scenes at the Family Guy studios to offer a peek at a production week of the series to see what goes into the making of the show.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Chris Has Got a Date, Date, Date, Date, Date
programme.desc: Taylor Swift agrees to go to a homecoming dance with Chris after he asks her via online video. However, when she starts to fall for him, her music takes a hit. Meanwhile, Peter decides to try a new profession and sees firsthand what it's like to be an Uber driver.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Mayor West raises the drinking age to 50, leaving Brian - thanks to dog years - the only one old enough to buy alcohol for Peter.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley finds herself pushed aside when Jeff's racing greyhound Fleabiscuit wins the Chimdale Stakes. Klaus, believing that the mechanical rabbit Wizbo is too plain looking, sets out to give it a little more personality and ends up going for a wild ride.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Stan lies about his texting and driving accident, he takes a seizure medication and suffers from the side effects, while Roger tries to help Steve overcome performance anxiety.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland accepts an offer from his boss to quit his job with six months' severance pay, but has difficulty telling Donna what he has done. Meanwhile, Rallo finds a handsome suitor for Roberta after Federline forgets their anniversary.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna discovers there is a rat in the house and moves herself and the children in with her mother.
