programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.sub-title: The Silent World of Nicholas Quinn
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar as detectives investigating a cold-case killing from almost 40 years earlier. The discovery of a skeleton in the cellar of a demolished building launches a murder hunt, headed up by DCI Cassie Stuart and DS Sunny Khan. However, all they have to go on is a car key found near the body, which leads them on a search to find out who he is, as well as how, when and why he died. WithTom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Ruth Sheen, Gemma Jones and Hannah Gordon.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Having identified the body in the cellar as Jimmy Sullivan, Cassie visits the victim's mother, who is relieved that she might finally find out what happened to her son 39 years after he went missing. The detectives focus their investigation on the names in Jimmy's diary, who are revealed to be a government advisor, a priest, a retired bookkeeper and a housewife with a secret past. What part, if any, could these four wildly different people have played in Jimmy's disappearance and demise? Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star in the crime drama, with Tom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill and Ruth Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis notices a rift developing between Rosie and Max after their honeymoon and decides to help them settle their differences. A farmer captures an endangered and vicious wild dog, but when the expert called in to locate the pack turns out to be Alice's former partner, she is torn between giving their relationship another chance and staying at Leopard's Den with Danny. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and Â£400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson.(n)

programme.desc: Alf becomes pompous about his OBE. Tricia keeps her house untidy to put off potential buyers. Vera is furious to discover Jack hasn't paid the council tax for three months.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma forgets her and Mike's anniversary. Martin is given a six-month contract at the hospital. Steve takes Fiona out for an expensive meal with the money Vicky gave him.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The leader of a censorship campaign comes to Oxford on a speaking tour, and Morse is charged with protecting her in light of a series of death threats. A bricklayer working on a controversial pop singer's house is later found dead, and when a second killing occurs, a connection emerges to the speaker's movement, plunging Morse into the middle of a conflict over the nature of British culture.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Dry Bones That Dream - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Dry Bones That Dream - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and Â£400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: When the Green Woods Laugh - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are accused of stealing milk, but Zak protests their innocence. Alan tells Frank that only he can restore harmony to the village. Terry gets stood up by Helen.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is still bitter with Frank for letting the Dingles move back into their house. Kathy tries to cheer Nick up. Nellie organises a party to celebrate the Dingles' homecoming.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The local press takes an interest in the missing money and try to interview Alf. Jack discovers Vera hasn't paid the mortgage for two months.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Robertses are horrified when the Gazette accuses Alf of being a thief. Ken is stunned when Tracy discharges herself from hospital. Vicky has a shock in store for Steve.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: By the Pricking of My Thumbs
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby splashes out on a new sports car in the hope of impressing Miss Jones, but a cruel practical joke by Philip convinces him he has run over his feline friend Vienna. Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour star.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.sub-title: The Silent World of Nicholas Quinn
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar as detectives investigating a cold-case killing from almost 40 years earlier. The discovery of a skeleton in the cellar of a demolished building launches a murder hunt, headed up by DCI Cassie Stuart and DS Sunny Khan. However, all they have to go on is a car key found near the body, which leads them on a search to find out who he is, as well as how, when and why he died. WithTom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Ruth Sheen, Gemma Jones and Hannah Gordon.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Having identified the body in the cellar as Jimmy Sullivan, Cassie visits the victim's mother, who is relieved that she might finally find out what happened to her son 39 years after he went missing. The detectives focus their investigation on the names in Jimmy's diary, who are revealed to be a government advisor, a priest, a retired bookkeeper and a housewife with a secret past. What part, if any, could these four wildly different people have played in Jimmy's disappearance and demise? Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star in the crime drama, with Tom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill and Ruth Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis notices a rift developing between Rosie and Max after their honeymoon and decides to help them settle their differences. A farmer captures an endangered and vicious wild dog, but when the expert called in to locate the pack turns out to be Alice's former partner, she is torn between giving their relationship another chance and staying at Leopard's Den with Danny. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Gloomy new tenant Mr Gray moves into Rigsby's boarding house, but proves so depressed that he barely seems to notice the dingy surroundings, and keeps talking about 'going to a better place' - raising fears he is contemplating suicide. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and David Swift.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera decides she doesn't want to work anymore. Tracy blames Ken for the mess they're all in, and Steve is attacked by an assailant with a baseball bat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim doesn't tell Liz the truth about what happened to Steve. Denise advises Fiona to leave Steve before he drags her down with him, and he is forced to tell Vicky about his debts.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The bailiffs arrive at the Dingles'. Linda tells Biff she doesn't deserve him, and Frank tries to stop Chris being arrested.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is upset that everyone seems to be on the Dingles' side. The villagers begin donating food to the Dingles, while Frank and Kim are made to feel unwelcome at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason visits a travellers' camp to investigate attacks on two hippies, and learns one of them had recently clashed with a local gamekeeper - who is later found murdered. Meanwhile, David suspects his taxi has been sabotaged, and Wetherby begins following his wife - only to discover she is being unfaithful. Joe McFadden and David Lonsdale star.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: The Darling Buds of May - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Taxman Charley decides to escape from the office and go strawberry-picking with Mariette and the rest of the Larkin family - a move that leaves his career hanging in the balance. Light-hearted drama based on the novels by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and Â£400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson.(n)

programme.desc: Alf becomes pompous about his OBE. Tricia keeps her house untidy to put off potential buyers. Vera is furious to discover Jack hasn't paid the council tax for three months.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma forgets her and Mike's anniversary. Martin is given a six-month contract at the hospital. Steve takes Fiona out for an expensive meal with the money Vicky gave him.(n)

programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and Â£400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The leader of a censorship campaign comes to Oxford on a speaking tour, and Morse is charged with protecting her in light of a series of death threats. A bricklayer working on a controversial pop singer's house is later found dead, and when a second killing occurs, a connection emerges to the speaker's movement, plunging Morse into the middle of a conflict over the nature of British culture.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Dry Bones That Dream - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Dry Bones That Dream - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars.(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.sub-title: Stacks of Cash and a Hidden Stash
programme.desc: US reality series following the exploits of ace motor dealer Jeff Allen and his team in Lubbock, Texas, known as the Flat 12. Jeff and Meg go on a spending spree in California, where a 1931 Hot Rod and a 1969 Camaro attract their attention.(n)

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: News and action from the world of motorsport.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Alex Riley and Vicki Butler-Henderson choose their favourite car from 1984, then try to convince the panel of judges that their choice is best.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley champion cars from 1955. Vicki chooses the Mercedes 190SL, while Alex plumps for the British MGA sports car.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose cars from 1980. Vicki likes the Audi Quattro, while Alex goes for a Renault 5 Turbo.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How traditional seaside rock is made, a process that has not changed since Victorian times. Plus, how a new fire extinguisher is saving lives around the world. Jimmy Nail narrates.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Jimmy Nail narrates a look inside a firework factory, before a lesson in pork pies in Melton Mowbray. Plus, how respective experts create fishing rods and single malt whisky.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Bombay Sapphire distillery to discover how exotic botanicals from around the world are used to flavour this classic British gin.(n)

programme.title: The Far Country (1954)
programme.desc: A cowpoke is steering his cattle north to the Canadian border when a corrupt sheriff commandeers the herd. A local saloon-bar owner falls for the hapless cowboy, and with her help he recaptures his cattle. Hooking up with a wagon train, the couple head for the mining town of Dawson, but the thwarted and embittered sheriff is on their tail. Western, with James Stewart, Walter Brennan and Ruth Roman.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Lawman (1971)

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's car lot is threatened with closure, spurring him to enter the cut-and-thrust world of local politics to have more of a say in local issues. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Lennard Pearce, best remembered as Grandad in Only Fools and Horses.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad trails diamond thief Jimmy Fleet on the inside, with Carter acting undercover as a fellow prisoner aiming to gain his confidence. On his release for lack of evidence, Fleet seeks Carter's assistance after discovering his girlfriend has vanished with the jewels, suspecting she has been abducted. Michael Elphick guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Quincy stumbles upon a plot devised by a group of Nazi war criminals to use an Egyptian mummy to smuggle rare gems from Cairo to Los Angeles.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: An undercover cop kills a Puerto Rican teenager in self-defence, leading to demands for his dismissal from the Hispanic community and the threat of violence on the streets.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a twisted tale of rock 'n' roll, meditation and murder. Anna Carteret and Trevor Bannister guest star, with Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying a dodgy greyhound and lumbered with a pile of knocked-off videos, and it is up to Terry to save him from a fate worse than death. Vintage drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Patrick Malahide.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a CIA agent dies after being thrown off a balcony, the unit sets up a fake arms deal with the German terrorist cell responsible. However, the gunrunners uncover the ploy, kidnap Bodie and transform him into a walking time-bomb. Lewis Collins stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Guest stars Eric Morecambe and Ernie Wise are entrusted to guard a consignment of heart pills by the girlfriend of the eminent research chemist developing the medicine. Before long, the comedy duo find themselves pursued by Middle Eastern operatives intent on procuring the new drug, as well as Sweeney officers who are trying to protect them. With John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by guest angler Gary Newman as they head to Croatia, where they target a rare 30lb grass carp. They also venture onto a wild river that will test their skills to the limit as they look to land a huge king carp.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Bold Contender and Skipper Squizzy head south to Port Davey for one last roll of the dice, but Glenn and the crew of Ansons Bay are also fishing in the same area.(n)

programme.title: Rocky III (1982)
programme.desc: World heavyweight boxing champion Rocky Balboa settles down into a life of luxury - but a cloud appears on the horizon in the shape of ambitious contender Clubber Lang, whose brutal fighting style represents a serious threat to his title. Drama, starring Sylvester Stallone, Mr T, Burgess Meredith, Talia Shire, Burt Young, Carl Weathers and Hulk Hogan.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Gambled with Life
programme.desc: An engineer hatches a plan to immortalise people by freezing them and bringing them back to life, and decides Simon Templar would make the ideal guinea pig.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: Give Us This Day Arthur Daley's Bread
programme.desc: Arthur employs a truck-load of ex-cons as cheap labour for his new landscape gardening business and things go swimmingly - until a series of thefts puts paid to the enterprise and he finds himself out of pocket. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle considers his future in CI5 after it transpires the father of his fiancee is under surveillance by the unit, suspected of being part of an international drug-smuggling ring. Patricia Hodge guest stars, with Martin Shaw.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An apparently respected gynaecologist's suitcase is stolen, but he is reluctant to reveal its contents. On further investigation the squad learns he was in the process of smuggling untaxed money out of the country and that a glamorous Latin woman with criminal connections, may hold the key to its whereabouts.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Featuring a family farm making jam for the Queen's breakfast table, a Sheffield man producing classic safety razors, a bed-maker using recycled bottles to make mattresses, and a British clockmaker paying tribute to a man that changed the course of history. Ricky Tomlinson narrates.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Bold Contender and Skipper Squizzy head south to Port Davey for one last roll of the dice, but Glenn and the crew of Ansons Bay are also fishing in the same area.(n)

programme.title: The Untouchables (1987)
programme.desc: Incorruptible federal agent Eliot Ness is assigned the task of fighting organised crime in Prohibition-era Chicago and bringing the notorious crime boss Al Capone to justice. Aware crooked police are undermining his investigations, he puts together his own elite squad to take on the Mob. Brian De Palma's crime thriller, with Kevin Costner, Robert De Niro, Andy Garcia and Sean Connery, who won an Oscar for his performance.(n)

programme.title: Rocky IV (1985)
programme.desc: The Italian Stallion agrees to help former champion Apollo Creed train for a match against a seemingly invincible boxer from the Soviet Union, only to see his friend die as a result of injuries sustained in the fight. Rocky vows to avenge him, and prepares to take on the Russian heavyweight in his own country. Drama sequel, directed by and starring Sylvester Stallone. With Dolph Lundgren, Talia Shire, Carl Weathers and Brigitte Nielsen.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars.(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.sub-title: Stacks of Cash and a Hidden Stash
programme.desc: US reality series following the exploits of ace motor dealer Jeff Allen and his team in Lubbock, Texas, known as the Flat 12. Jeff and Meg go on a spending spree in California, where a 1931 Hot Rod and a 1969 Camaro attract their attention.(n)

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: News and action from the world of motorsport.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Alex Riley and Vicki Butler-Henderson choose their favourite car from 1984, then try to convince the panel of judges that their choice is best.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley champion cars from 1955. Vicki chooses the Mercedes 190SL, while Alex plumps for the British MGA sports car.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose cars from 1980. Vicki likes the Audi Quattro, while Alex goes for a Renault 5 Turbo.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How traditional seaside rock is made, a process that has not changed since Victorian times. Plus, how a new fire extinguisher is saving lives around the world. Jimmy Nail narrates.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Jimmy Nail narrates a look inside a firework factory, before a lesson in pork pies in Melton Mowbray. Plus, how respective experts create fishing rods and single malt whisky.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Bombay Sapphire distillery to discover how exotic botanicals from around the world are used to flavour this classic British gin.(n)

programme.title: The Far Country (1954)
programme.desc: A cowpoke is steering his cattle north to the Canadian border when a corrupt sheriff commandeers the herd. A local saloon-bar owner falls for the hapless cowboy, and with her help he recaptures his cattle. Hooking up with a wagon train, the couple head for the mining town of Dawson, but the thwarted and embittered sheriff is on their tail. Western, with James Stewart, Walter Brennan and Ruth Roman.(n)

With John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Teen Wolf (1985)
programme.desc: A teenage misfit is initially dismayed to learn he is a werewolf as a result of a hereditary curse. He finds there are advantages as he becomes a local celebrity, while his enhanced agility makes him the star player of his school's basketball team - but fame quickly goes to his head. Comedy, starring Michael J Fox, James Hampton and Susan Ursitti.(n)

Action adventure sequel, starring Megan Fox, Will Arnett and Stephen Amell.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: Comedy entertainment show written by Harry Hill, with Matt celebrating everything that is keeping the public, and some celebrity chums, entertained at home. The Little Britain star showcases the ability of men, women, children, celebrities and possibly even some pets to amuse themselves during this self-enforced downtime, as he's joined by a plethora of key workers, families at home and famous faces. Special guests appearing on the first episode include singer Ronan Keating, comedian Suzi Ruffell, newsreader Krishnan Guru-Murthy, family favourite Basil Brush, radio host Roman Kemp, Made in Chelsea star Jamie Laing and comedian Judi Love.(n)

programme.title: When Will Lockdown End?
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken explores why the coronavirus lockdown is continuing, as he seeks answers to the questions many people have about when lockdown will end and what the world will look like by then. Will people who have had the virus be allowed more freedom of movement than those who haven't? What kind of restrictions will we have on work and movement, and for how long? Xand looks at both sides of the debate, meeting scientists, experts and members of the government; and also shares helpful advice on how to cope best with the challenges of the situation we face.(n)

programme.title: Homeland
programme.desc: Saul uses back channels, and Carrie needs one more favour. Thriller following a team of CIA agents battling terrorists and problems in their personal lives. Claire Danes stars.(n)

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: At this time of national crisis, Grayson Perry sets out to bring the nation together through art. Every week, the Turner Prize-winning artist hosts the show from his studio, where he creates new pieces, leads masterclasses and finds out how other artists, creatives and celebrities have been spending their time in isolation. He also encourages artists and viewers alike to produce visual representations of this unique time, at the end of which he plans to display the art in an exhibition chronicling the changing moods of Britain in isolation. The theme for the opening edition is portraits of a family member or housemate, with comedian Joe Lycett showing off one he has painted.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The training focuses on the recruits' mindset and the 12 famous faces are given a set of mental and physical challenges to see if they have what it takes to be a special forces operative. A brutal night-time beasting sees the first celeb quit the course, before there is a backwards helicopter dive into near freezing waters, a psychological test in a simulated warzone and a session of murderball. As the challenges get tougher and the recruits' weaknesses are exposed, their past experiences and emotions come to the surface.(n)

programme.title: The Mum Who Got Tourette's
programme.desc: Elizabeth is a typical suburban housewife and mother of three with one big difference - without warning, shortly after her 40th birthday, she developed Tourette's syndrome - a neurological condition that causes involuntary tics, some of them offensive. This one-off documentary follows Elizabeth and her family over the course of a busy summer, including a holiday in Cornwall, GCSE results and meeting her son's new girlfriend - experiences amplified by the unexpected impact of Elizabeth's condition.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: Comedy entertainment show written by Harry Hill, with Matt celebrating everything that is keeping the public, and some celebrity chums, entertained at home. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackpool compete for the Â£1,000 prize, beginning with practising Buddhist Antonio and former redcoat Jason, who serve up a sushi starter. programme.title: Can Science Beat the Virus?
programme.desc: A special debate about the coronavirus pandemic, with only scientists on the panel. As Britain increasingly looks beyond politics to science for reassurance about how and when the crisis will end, this programme brings together experts to take part in a straight-talking, fact-based discussion providing scientific answers to the most difficult questions. The discussion covers the psychology of isolation, the technical issues behind mass production of ventilators, and, crucially, gives viewers the chance to explore the fascinating science behind this extraordinary outbreak.(n)

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: A patient comes into the surgery for a further examination after a prostate cancer screening, and a woman suffering from pain after the removal of a contraceptive coil is treated. Elsewhere, Dr Qamar has an appointment with teenager Laila, who has been experiencing painful headaches on a daily basis.(n)

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Michael Buerk, Shaun Williamson, Amanda Barrie, Anita Harris and John Prescott trade the countryside for cityscapes, drifting toward the industrial heart of the potteries in Stoke-on-Trent. As well as learning about the area's rich history along the way, they share some revelations of their own - such as who has showered the least, how to eat mushy peas without cutlery, and why ducks are scared of journalists. It's not all plain sailing, however, and after spending a rough few nights sleeping in the kitchen, John abandons ship to find an alternative.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor enjoys a 10,000km journey along the Trans-Siberian Railway, a network of railways connecting Moscow with the Russian Far East. In Khabarovsk, Tony is met by the extraordinary sight of the River Amur, frozen solid. He's invited to warm up over dinner with a group of locals who offer him a taste of their home-brewed vodka. Tony then re-joins the rails, learning about how Russia has changed since the days of communism. Further stops on his tour are Yekaterinburg, in the foothills of the Ural Mountains and Red Square in Moscow, before he heads west towards Finland's capital city, Helsinki.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a high-speed police interception unit, riding along with officers covering the West Yorkshire region, which includes Leeds, Bradford and Wakefield.(n)

programme.title: Blood

programme.title: Yorkshire Ripper: Left for Dead
programme.desc: Peter Sutcliffe is one of the most notorious British serial killers of all time, murdering 13 women and attempting to kill at least eight more during a five-year reign of terror during the late 1970s leading into 1980. This documentary tells the experiences of women who survived attacks by him, revealing that the police's failure to take notice of them may have had an impact on the investigation.(n)

programme.title: Red Dragon (2002)
programme.desc: The FBI agent who first captured Hannibal Lecter is called out of retirement to catch a demented murderer dubbed the Tooth Fairy, who slaughters a family whenever there is a full moon - forcing the detective to draw on the imprisoned cannibal's expertise in criminal psychology. Thriller prequel adapted from the first of Thomas Harris's Lecter novels, starring Anthony Hopkins, Edward Norton, Ralph Fiennes and Philip Seymour Hoffman.(n)

programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents the life and work of the Western world's most gifted artists, beginning with Giotto di Bondone, the son of a Tuscan shepherd. programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Documentary following life at Julian Norton and Peter Wright's veterinary practice as they and their team care for animals in the rural communities of North Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Michael Buerk, Amanda Barrie, Anita Harris and John Prescott set off for a spooky trip through Staffordshire's 1.5-mile-long Harecastle Tunnel. However, Shaun Williamson opts instead to visit the grave of James Brindley, the man who designed the tunnel, but never saw it completed. After joining the Macclesfield Canal, Amanda and Anita head to a barge-based artist's studio for a gentle afternoon of sketching, while Shaun and Michael head to 18th-century folly Mow Cop. This leg of the journey ends at Congleton, homing in on an unsung local heroine of the Suffragette movement.(n)

programme.title: Dogs with Incredible Jobs
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dogs with unusual skills around the world who put their talents to use helping humans and other animals. Among the many animals featured in the series are truffle hunters, bedbug detectors, police dogs, emotional support dogs, sheep dogs, guide dogs, sled pullers, fire fighters, social media stars, mice harvesters, fitness trainers, circus performers, frisbee gamers, trauma counsellors, penguin protectors, tracking dogs, prison guards and snake detectors.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Julian treats a lhasa apso with a mouth tumour which other vets have given up on, and comes up with an alternative course of action to save the dog's life. He also locks horns with an aggressive ram that has a sore eye. Over in Thirsk, Peter treats a Shetland pony with a painful foot condition and a cow with a prolapsed uterus.(n)

programme.title: Blood
programme.desc: Fiona's interrogation by the police continues as she recounts the days leading up to the twins' communion. Paul must choose between helping Kian and protecting his friends, while Jim's efforts to rebuild his life are met with resistance.(n)

programme.title: Missing or Murdered?
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the case of 19-year-old Luke Durbin, who vanished in May 2006 after a night out in Ipswich. He was captured on CCTV seemingly trying to find his way home, but he had left his phone and wallet at a friend's house, so taxis refused to take him and there were no buses or trains. For the first time, details about his involvement in drugs and drug-dealing are revealed. Could his disappearance be linked to a turf war?(n)

programme.title: Killer Psychopaths
programme.desc: David Wilson analyses the personality and motivations of serial killer Steve Wright, who was convicted in 2008 of murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich, and became known as the Suffolk Strangler. With the aid of first-hand accounts from those involved at the time, including investigating officers, journalists and the killer's father, the criminologist reveals how the dark side of Wright's personality eventually took over.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie (2004)
programme.desc: The plucky sea creature sets out to prove his boss Mr Krabs is innocent of stealing Neptune's crown. However, the missing item has ended up in sinister Shell City, which all sea-dwellers fear and from which few have ever returned. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny, Bill Fagerbakke, Clancy Brown and Scarlett Johansson, plus a cameo appearance by David Hasselhoff.(n)

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs.(n)

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio.(n)

programme.title: Sahara (2005)
programme.desc: An explorer stumbles upon evidence that an American Civil War battleship, which was laden with treasure when it sank without trace, has ended up somewhere in the Sahara. However, he is deflected from his mercenary quest to find the hoard by an encounter with a tenacious doctor, whose battle to stop the spread of a deadly disease could be linked to his own mission. Adventure, starring Matthew McConaughey, Penelope Cruz and Steve Zahn.(n)

programme.title: Transporter 3 (2008)
programme.desc: Underworld courier Frank Martin's latest assignment involves transporting the kidnapped daughter of a crooked government minister across Europe on behalf of an eco-terrorist faction. However, a device will kill him if he leaves his car, thugs employed by the politician are out to murder him, and his passenger has a bad case of Stockholm syndrome. Action thriller sequel, with Jason Statham and Natalya Rudakova.(n)

programme.title: Sabotage (2014)
programme.desc: The members of an elite Drug Enforcement Administration team secretly keep the millions of dollars they find during the raid. When they are killed off one by one, the leader becomes convinced one of his men is getting rid of the others to keep all the money, and sets out to expose the murderer. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Sam Worthington.(n)

programme.title: August: Osage County (2013)
programme.desc: Three estranged sisters return to their family home following their father's suicide, and resume their complex relationship with their overbearing, malicious mother. Over the day of the funeral, tensions among the family reach boiling point as secrets and recriminations are brought to light. Drama, starring Meryl Streep, Julia Roberts, Juliette Lewis and Julianne Nicholson.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.sub-title: BSL Zone: The Muddy Boot Room
programme.desc: Adam Bassett and his guests discuss deaf football, on and off the pitch.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Constable (1960)
programme.desc: A flu epidemic leaves a police station short of staff, forcing a desperate inspector to send four bungling trainees on the beat. The inept would-be bobbies manage to increase the crime rate even before they have started work, and their long-suffering sergeant faces an uphill battle to whip them into shape. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims.(n)

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1959)
programme.desc: An innocent man becomes embroiled in international espionage as he tries to uncover the meaning behind the cryptic last words of a mystery woman, whom he knew for only a brief time before finding her dying in his flat. He must flee for his life from foreign spies and the police, who think he is a murderer. Spy thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Kenneth More, Taina Elg, Barry Jones and James Hayter.(n)

programme.title: Charade (1963)
programme.desc: A distraught widow learns her husband was murdered for his part in a $250,000 robbery. The haul has gone missing, and his former accomplices are soon on her tail in the hunt for the missing proceeds. Not knowing where to turn, she takes up the offer of help from a dashing stranger, but is uncertain whether she can trust him. Romantic thriller, with Audrey Hepburn, Cary Grant, Walter Matthau and James Coburn.(n)

programme.title: Short Circuit (1986)
programme.desc: A military robot is struck by lightning and develops a mind of its own. The machine embarks on a series of comic misadventures, but the company that made him wants him back. He realises his only hope is to convince the scientist who invented him that he really is alive. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Steve Guttenberg, Ally Sheedy and Fisher Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul.(n)

programme.title: Daddy's Home 2 (2017)
programme.desc: Having settled their differences, Brad and Dusty's newfound friendship is put to the test when their respective fathers - one gentle, the other overly macho - arrive for the Christmas holidays, forcing them to change their plans. What starts as a break at a fun-filled trip to a luxury resort turns into something far more chaotic. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Mark Wahlberg, Mel Gibson and John Lithgow.(n)

programme.title: God's Own Country (2017)
programme.desc: Young Yorkshire farmer Johnny Saxby numbs his frustrations with casual sex and alcohol. However, his life changes when he falls for a Romanian migrant worker during lambing season. Writer/director Francis Lee's drama, starring Josh O'Connor, Alec Secareanu and Gemma Jones.(n)

programme.title: Bunny and the Bull (2009)
programme.desc: A man suffering from agoraphobia is unable to leave his flat. When his food runs out, he is forced to contemplate rejoining the outside world and starts to relive the events of a traumatic holiday he spent travelling across Europe with his lecherous former best friend, which led to his current condition. Comedy, starring Edward Hogg, Simon Farnaby and Veronia Echegui.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.sub-title: BSL Zone: The Muddy Boot Room
programme.desc: Adam Bassett and his guests discuss deaf football, on and off the pitch.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Regardless (1961)
programme.desc: A businessman starts up an odd-job agency, but employs seven hopeless handymen incapable of completing any task without causing utter chaos. Matters come to a head when their landlord tries to evict them - forcing the bungling workmen to finally prove their worth. Slapstick comedy, starring Sid James, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams.(n)

programme.title: The Ladykillers (1955)
programme.desc: A criminal mastermind and his gang come up with a cunning scheme to steal a fortune, but their plan is threatened by the unsuspecting elderly landlady of the boarding house they are staying in. They decide to dispose of her, which proves easier said than done. Classic Ealing comedy, with Alec Guinness, Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Katie Johnson, Cecil Parker, Danny Green and Jack Warner.(n)

programme.title: All That Heaven Allows (1955)
programme.desc: A wealthy New England widow breaks all the taboos of her conservative social circle by falling madly in love with her younger gardener. Her friends and grown-up children are scandalised as she contemplates marrying him, leaving her with a dilemma - to either bow to convention or take a chance at being happy. Romantic drama, with Jane Wyman, Rock Hudson, Agnes Moorehead and Conrad Nagel.(n)

programme.title: Chasing Mavericks (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager discovers a supposedly legendary stretch of shoreline famous for vast waves actually exists. He tracks down a famous surfer and asks for help in developing his skills to the point where he can successfully ride the challenging waters. Fact-based drama, starring Jonny Weston, Gerard Butler and Elisabeth Shue.(n)

programme.title: Footloose (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager from Boston moves to a small conservative town, and discovers the council and a preacher have enforced a ban on loud music and dancing. He openly defies the harsh rules, revitalising the local youth and winning the heart of the reverend's daughter. Remake of the 1984 drama, with Kenny Wormald, Julianne Hough and Dennis Quaid.(n)

programme.title: Chaos (2005)
programme.desc: A veteran cop and his inexperienced new partner are assigned to investigate a bank heist. At first sight, no money appears to have been stolen. However, it eventually emerges the criminal mastermind behind the robbery had a more complex and profitable scheme in mind - and a possible connection to the detective on his trail. Crime thriller, starring Jason Statham, Ryan Phillippe and Wesley Snipes.(n)

programme.title: Before I Go to Sleep (2014)
programme.desc: A woman who cannot retain her memories following a traumatic accident is contacted by a mysterious neuropsychologist, who offers to help her recover the truth about her condition. As she tries to piece together the events of her past, she begins to fear neither her husband nor the doctor are who they seem to be. Thriller, starring Nicole Kidman, Colin Firth and Mark Strong.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Newbury Park in southern California to help Mexican restaurant Mama Rita's. The owner previously ran a successful catering company but has struggled to adapt her skills to meet the demands of a new business - and with a year's worth of food stored in the freezer Gordon realises he has a tough job ahead.(n)

programme.desc: Impulsive groom Steve plans a cliff-hanger of a Wild West wedding, but demanding bride-to-be Leona dreams of a more sophisticated do.(n)

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi have a disagreement at the office and must prevent it from becoming a problem at home, whilethe kids try to extend Lowell's stay after realising he is an amazing cook.(n)

programme.title: Man with a Plan
