programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: It's July 12th and the Quinn family are rushing to get out of town before the Orange Parades start. They are packed and nearly ready to go when James, Michelle and Clare arrive as unexpected members of the escape party. To make matters worse, Aunt Sarah has recently started reading tarot cards and is predicting disaster for this trip - and it seems that for once, she might just be right. The only question is, when and how will things go wrong? Comedy, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Jamie-Lee O'Donnell, Dylan Llewellyn and Nicola Coughlan.(n)

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin is over the moon when she manages to secure herself the editorship of the school magazine, The Habit. Especially when she uncovers an explosive story. Orla become obsessed by step aerobics, Da Gerry gets into trouble with Ma Mary and love is in the air for Aunt Sarah. Comedy, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Louisa Harland, Tommy Tiernan, Tara Lynne O'Neill and Kathy Kiera Clarke.(n)

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Moss has his work cut out protecting Jen from the unwanted attentions of would-be suitor Bill Crouse. Meanwhile, Roy also struggles to fend off an admirer. Comedy, starring Katherine Parkinson, Chris O'Dowd and Richard Ayoade, with Adam Buxton.(n)

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Jen explains she experiences terrible mood swings at a certain time of the month, but when Moss and Roy start displaying similar symptoms she begins to worry for their future - and that of the IT community in general.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend take on a 1934 Singer Le Mans two-seater sports car owned by 70-year-old farmer John from Cambridgeshire. The car's last outing was in 1998, when it had a starring role in a glorious wedding. The boys have to retrieve the car from a dusty barn, but once they have it back at the workshop, they discover the engine is in a very poor state and find they may have to replace it.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Car enthusiast and Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend visit Plymouth to rescue a 25-year-old example of the world's bestselling two-seater sports car - the Mazda MX5. The particular motor belongs to former ambulance driver Paul and has been off the road for some time. With a '90s Japanese sports car in the workshop, Tim cannot let the chance to try out a few modifications go by.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Cambridge to rescue a cherished 1991 Range Rover Vogue SE, which is in need of some TLC.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nursing manager Sarah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, taking inspiration from her husband's Middle Eastern heritage to offer her guests a Turkish fusion menu. Joining her is 28-year-old painter decorator Alex, 45-year-old bridal shop owner Abbi, 60-year-old community centre manager Janet and 56-year-old record label boss Phil. A hilarious evening lies in store, with unexpected tattoos, beauty tips and twerking.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Would-be clairvoyant Janet hosts the second of a week of dinner parties in Leeds and hopes her ghoul-themed dinner party will help her win the cash prize. Part of Janet's plan is to pretend her house is haunted and she has a practical joke lined up for nursing manager Sarah and bridal shop owner Abbi to find. With more surprises in store, will the fright-night theme be enough to ghost Janet into the lead?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Record label owner Phil hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, hoping to win the Â£1,000 prize money by serving up a 1970s rock-themed menu. His guests are astounded by his mystery past, and painter and decorator Alex is unimpressed when wedding shop owner Abbi continues to wind him up.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Painter and decorator Alex hosts the fourth night in Leeds, hoping to win the prize money by serving up a cruise-themed menu from around the world - which impresses jealous rival Janet so much, she almost turns violent. Abbi continues to belittle the host - until he reveals he spends his evenings on stage impersonating a famous heart-throb singer. Will his performance bag him the top prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bridal shop owner Abbi hopes to woo her guests with a wedding-themed party on the final day of the competition in Leeds. All week Abbi and Michael Buble impersonator Alex have had a bit of a banter that has sailed close to the wind. Will the tension take over, or can the wedding theme create an amorous atmosphere that will help Abbi win the Â£1,000 prize?(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Kent, 51-year-old construction site manager and part-time bouncer Mark hopes that his dinner party can be as much about the party as it is about the dinner, as he hosts a cheesy fun-fuelled evening. Will party poppers, balloons, confetti cannons and surprising celebrity stories impress 53-year-old dog-lover Karen, 39-year-old Czech shop assistant Verush, 42-year-old singer Amrick and 58-year-old fashion photographer Anthony?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Kent, competitive sales assistant Verush is going all out to win by hosting a Czech-themed evening, but ex-stripper Mark isn't impressed, comparing Verush's pasta to MDF and pastry to leather, while the first night's argument between dog lover Karen and photographer Anthony is reignited.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog enthusiast Karen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Kent, hoping to impress her guests by serving up a menu inspired by her four-legged friends. After the cheesy fun of the previous night, she is determined to impress her guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amrick takes his turn to host in Kent, hoping to wow his guests by revealing his musical talents and with his traditional Indian cuisine. But with dog-lover Karen and competitive Verush not liking hot and spicy food, there could be trouble - while photographer Anthony questions ex-stripper Mark's dress sense.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Anthony attempts to wow his guests with an evening of fine food and culinary excellence on the final day of the competition in Kent. However, the soiree is jeopardised by ex-stripper Mark on overdrive and dog-lover Karen showcasing some incredible impressions from animals to Anthony himself. The host might be cockily confident he can win, but Karen seemingly remains in pole position to bag the Â£1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of 37-year-old Angelo Mastropietro, as he transforms a damp, dark and abandoned cave in Worcestershire. Having recently been diagnosed with multiple sclerosis, Angelo wants to create a retreat to help him cope with the news and become a 21st-century caveman. He has a budget of Â£100,000 and plans to almost single-handedly carve, cut and drill into the hillside to make the cave comfortable for contemporary living. However, there is no manual for a project of this kind, and the unique challenge soon becomes an obsession.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Bromsgrove treat a 52-year-old man suffering sudden, severe headaches, while Manni Suman and Lucy McGraynor look after an unconscious patient.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: A look at the impact on the emergency services of the rising number of rough sleepers, with Wiltshire police's call handlers dealing with complaints about the homeless every day. In Swindon, PC Dan Travers is sent to deal with reports from a concerned resident regarding a homeless man sitting on her wall, while PC Hannah Agate is called to the 10th floor of a multistorey car park, where a man is threatening to jump after he and his family were evicted after falling behind on the rent.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-nine-year-old Carole is brought in with signs of a stroke, and neurologist Dr Mahinda opts for a treatment that only has a slim chance of success. A 77-year-old man with diabetes has an infection in his toes, and doctors consider amputating his foot to stop it spreading. A woman's false eyelashes leave her with abrasions to her eyes.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Bromsgrove treat a 52-year-old man suffering sudden, severe headaches, while Manni Suman and Lucy McGraynor look after an unconscious patient.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-nine-year-old Carole is brought in with signs of a stroke, and neurologist Dr Mahinda opts for a treatment that only has a slim chance of success. A 77-year-old man with diabetes has an infection in his toes, and doctors consider amputating his foot to stop it spreading. A woman's false eyelashes leave her with abrasions to her eyes.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priest makes a valiant effort to give up cigarettes for Lent, inspired by a challenge from Dick Byrne, his opposite number over on Rugged Island, but soon finds he is not cut out for a life of self-denial. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp tries out classic recipes for Cornish pasties and Yorkshire puddings. She also meets pig farmer Jennie Whitely, who shows her how to successfully use all parts of the animal, and finds out what goes into brawn - a meat jelly made with flesh from a pig's head.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: A man who works abroad for a lot of the year and his partner want to live in the best possible environment to raise their two-year-old son. However, they cannot decide whether this is in Somerset or near Valencia in Spain. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton step in with some expert advice.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to rural Buckinghamshire to help Kat Dancer with her contemporary farmhouse. Kat has always wanted to build on the farm where she grew up and Charlie has bright ideas to make the most of the idyllic plot. However, when Kat is left to manage the project alone, the whole construction hangs in the balance.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows.(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's Greek Week, and the remaining contestants are challenged to sculpt a nude Greek statue and throw Greek jugs at the wheel. Judges Keith Brymer Jones and Sue Pryke decide who will be potter of the week, and who will be leaving the competition. Presented by Melanie Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree await the arrival of their guests, who are expecting a luxurious Christmas at the chateau. Dick continues work on installing the lift, but the project tests his ingenuity to the limit, while Angel finds more valuables in the attic, including a collection of vinyl records.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of teacher Michele Long and architect Michael Howe, who want to restore and transform a 100-year-old blacksmiths in Co Antrim. With virtually no practical experience, Michael wants to do as much of the project as possible by himself. However, the weather on the coastline is fierce, and the freezing weather coupled with a tight budget of 150k means the build is stretched from one to three years.(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.sub-title: How to Build British: Aston Martin
programme.desc: Documentary charting the step-by-step process of the construction of a DBS Superleggera luxury grand tourer sports car, from its oven-baked chassis to the special paint used.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-two-year-old retired firefighter John is airlifted to A&E after being accidentally hit in the face with a golf club, which hit him so hard that it snapped. With a suspected critical head injury, he is sent for a CT scan, and doctors are concerned that his right eye is not reacting as it should and he is weak down the right-hand side of his body. Meanwhile, 77-year-old Joan arrives in St George's after falling down the stairs at home and hurting her leg. As she waits to be treated, she talks about growing up in Camberwell.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Father Jack is hauled off to the local old priests' home to be treated for a strange medical complaint. He is replaced by a younger man - who proceeds to drive everyone crazy by playing jungle music at ear-splitting volume for hours on end. Dermot Morgan and Frank Kelly star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp learns the art of willow-weaving, taught by specialist Eddie Glew. The presenter also enters the hard craft category at the Chagford Show in Devon, where her fellow competitors create a mosaic elephant and landscape.(n)

programme.desc: Richard Fall and Laura Howes are thinking of settling down to start a family, but are torn between buying a large home in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, near where they live, or purchasing an apartment in Valencia on Spain's eastern coast.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The six remaining potters are challenged to produce handmade tiles to fit a fireplace surround and hearth, before throwing a chimney in the second challenge for a chance to secure a place in the quarter final. Hosted by Melanie Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Two-and-a-half years have passed since Dick and Angel Strawbridge swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only Â£280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs

With the help of one young builder and the knowledge they can learn from the internet, the couple plan to project manage the conversion themselves. However, they have only eight months and a budget of just over Â£200k to make a warm and comfortable home with straw bale walls and wide expanses of glass.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The surprising preventive and curative 'medicine' developed by certain species, including the hippopotamus, which secretes a red slime to act as a highly efficient sun screen.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Two families with remarkable powers of regenerating unusually large body parts easily - salamanders, which do so when a large chunk, such as the tail or a limb, has been bitten off by a predator, and male moose, which grow and shed antlers every year.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert.(n)

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

Their guests are Sinead from Northern Ireland and her prank-loving partner Tony, and competitive couple Kally and Mark. The lads bond over football, and by the end of the night Tony has played a prank or two setting the tone for the week. Subsequently, Kally and Mark put on an Anglo-Indian menu, but things soon get heated in the kitchen. Then, Tony and Sinead serve up an Irish menu, with silly dances, silly jokes and silly accents. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

Outspoken duo Kate and Ian don't let the evening's many delays go unnoticed, and as tensions rise around the table, internet lovebirds Chris and Amy try to stay firmly out of the way. Day two sees Kate and Ian serve up a menu full of rustic French cuisine, influenced by their 22 years living in France. But a table laying mishap suggests that Kate has overtly snubbed her guests, although tensions are eased when former falconers Kate and Ian bring out one of their favourite birds of prey. With tensions rising, the food not up to scratch and everyone playing the blame game, the chef is faced with one of the trickiest situations he's ever encountered.(n)

They are then asked to create their 'happy places' out of meringue for the showstopper challenge. Who will flap at the first hurdle, and who will battle their way through to be crowned star baker? Presented by Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding.(n)

programme.title: When Will Lockdown End?
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken explores why the coronavirus lockdown is continuing, as he seeks answers to the questions many people have about when lockdown will end and what the world will look like by then. Will people who have had the virus be allowed more freedom of movement than those who haven't? What kind of restrictions will we have on work and movement, and for how long? Xand looks at both sides of the debate, meeting scientists, experts and members of the government; and also shares helpful advice on how to cope best with the challenges of the situation we face.(n)

programme.desc: The 12 celebrities are tasked with a set of challenging mental and physical challenges to see if they have what it takes to be a special forces operative. A brutal night-time beasting sees the first celeb quit the course. There is a backwards helicopter dive into near-freezing waters and a psychological test in a simulated warzone, with a gun and a life-changing decision to make. YouTuber Jack Maynard is in the thick of it, as well as TV presenter Anthea Turner, while former footballer John Fashanu reflects on the suicide of his brother Justin.(n)

programme.title: The Mum Who Got Tourette's
programme.desc: Elizabeth is a typical suburban housewife and mother of three with one big difference - without warning, shortly after her 40th birthday, she developed Tourette's syndrome - a neurological condition that causes involuntary tics, some of them offensive. This one-off documentary follows Elizabeth and her family over the course of a busy summer, including a holiday in Cornwall, GCSE results and meeting her son's new girlfriend - experiences amplified by the unexpected impact of Elizabeth's condition.(n)

programme.title: When Will Lockdown End?
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken explores why the coronavirus lockdown is continuing, as he seeks answers to the questions many people have about when lockdown will end and what the world will look like by then. Will people who have had the virus be allowed more freedom of movement than those who haven't? What kind of restrictions will we have on work and movement, and for how long? Xand looks at both sides of the debate, meeting scientists, experts and members of the government; and also shares helpful advice on how to cope best with the challenges of the situation we face.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: At this time of national crisis, Grayson Perry sets out to bring the nation together through art. Every week, the Turner Prize-winning artist hosts the show from his studio, where he creates new pieces, leads masterclasses and finds out how other artists, creatives and celebrities have been spending their time in isolation. He also encourages artists and viewers alike to produce visual representations of this unique time, at the end of which he plans to display the art in an exhibition chronicling the changing moods of Britain in isolation.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp's weekly project is to design and make a name sign for her son's wall, and she shows how to make a cushion out of a jumper and tries her hand at Mexican tin art.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Darwin Lake Holiday Village in Matlock, Derbyshire, managed by Sally McAnulty and Teresa Shirley, who pride themselves on their exclusive village feel.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge searches for a stash of wine, said to be hidden by the Germans during the Second World War. Fiona starts renovating a dining room for a visit by the mayor. Philip and Angelina continue to prepare their chateau and its grounds for a big event, but they have a lot to do and are running out of time.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows how to make a robot dog out of tin cans and a terrarium, and sews a beach scene panel onto her cushion using a home-made cardboard loom.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Woodland Park, Colorado, where guests have a choice between casual fine-dining service or a pick-up from the drive-through takeaway. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired pair Retired Dawn and Les from Basingstoke want to relocate to sunny south-east Cyprus, where they're looking for a townhouse or bungalow that all four generations of their family can enjoy. The couple used to be leaders in the Scouts and the Brownies, so Jonnie Irwin has to work hard to earn his house hunting badge, as he comes up with five properties for their Â£100,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Darwin Lake Holiday Village in Matlock, Derbyshire, managed by Sally McAnulty and Teresa Shirley, who pride themselves on their exclusive village feel.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second day in and around Brighton, and bubbly 26-year-old Emma is hosting a beach-themed dinner party in the hope of winning the Â£1,000 cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Property expert Phil Spencer goes house-hunting in Hertfordshire on behalf of newlyweds Carl and Liz, who want to leave London for a new start in St Albans. Can he find them a home with space that is also well-connected for Â£425,000? Meanwhile, Kirstie Allsopp assists Naomi, who wants a place in Hemel Hempstead with enough room for her pole-dancing equipment.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray loses his wedding ring and is propositioned by an attractive woman at the airport, so a jealous Debra decides to get her own back - with disastrous results. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra try to conceal an embarrassing accident by telling Frank they fell down the stairs, sending him into a DIY frenzy that puts him in hospital.(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles clashes with girlfriend Mel over a misunderstanding, Daphne picks up the wrong signals, and friends are thin on the ground at Frasier's Christmas party. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip in the Winnebago on New Year's Eve to celebrate the turn of the new millennium, but a case of mistaken identity leaves Frasier and Martin trying to rescue Niles from another family's van.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ben and Ambika kick off the competition in Hertfordshire with a south-east Asian menu. Their guests are Sinead from Northern Ireland and her prank-loving partner Tony, and competitive couple Kally and Mark. The lads bond over football, and by the end of the night Tony has played a prank or two setting the tone for the week. Subsequently, Kally and Mark put on an Anglo-Indian menu, but things soon get heated in the kitchen. Then, Tony and Sinead serve up an Irish menu, with silly dances, silly jokes and silly accents. However, it remains to be seen which couple will pocket the prize for best soiree.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's crunch time for Jonathan and Michael who are facing their investors, but their newest idea - to keep goats - does not go down well. Philip and Angelina host the big anniversary party they've been spending weeks preparing for, but with broken toilets and 60 guests to contend with, how will it go? Plus, single mother Fiona battles to get her dining room ready to show off to the mayor.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: At this time of national crisis, Grayson Perry sets out to bring the nation together through art. Every week, the Turner Prize-winning artist hosts the show from his studio, where he creates new pieces, leads masterclasses and finds out how other artists, creatives and celebrities have been spending their time in isolation. He also encourages artists and viewers alike to produce visual representations of this unique time, at the end of which he plans to display the art in an exhibition chronicling the changing moods of Britain in isolation. The theme for the opening edition is portraits of a family member or housemate, with comedian Joe Lycett showing off one he has painted.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Documentary following archaeologists working to unlock the ancient secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings, beginning with the treasures of Tutankhamun's tomb. New technology discovers why the boy king's final resting place went undisturbed for so long, while elsewhere in the valley, a dig unearths a previously undiscovered 4000-year-old tomb.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill, Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp, actor and writer Roisin Conaty, and broadcaster Bill Turnbull take part in the charity contest. Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted challenges, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: Comedy entertainment show written by Harry Hill, with Matt celebrating everything that is keeping the public, and some celebrity chums, entertained at home. The Little Britain star showcases the ability of men, women, children, celebrities and possibly even some pets to amuse themselves during this self-enforced downtime, as he's joined by a plethora of key workers, families at home and famous faces. Special guests appearing on the first episode include singer Ronan Keating, comedian Suzi Ruffell, newsreader Krishnan Guru-Murthy, family favourite Basil Brush, radio host Roman Kemp, Made in Chelsea star Jamie Laing and comedian Judi Love.(n)

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Mum is in hospital having a 'woman's procedure', so Aunty Val is tasked with making the Friday night dinner. To Adam and Jonny's horror, she spends most of the evening glued to a dating app, before showing them several photos of semi-naked men. Val is especially obsessed with one guy, who goes by the name of Loverboy 309. The object of her lust is picking Val up from the house in 40 minutes. Thus begins an evening of hell for the Goodmans, as Val forces them to eat their lamb dinner in record time. Comedy, starring Paul Ritter, Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Tracy-Ann Oberman.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost Â£8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect Â£3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost Â£2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly Â£3,500 from a businessman in West London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly Â£3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe Â£750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase Â£30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay Â£2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover Â£45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a Â£4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A furious landlord discovers his tenant has been subletting his exclusive north-west London flat - having not paid any rent for months. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Documentary about the horrors of the rental market including a landlord who decided to sell his east London flat so he could afford a property with his girlfriend. But when he told his tenant, things quickly turned sour and the man stopped paying rent and refused to leave. Plus, the story of a pub owner in Lincolnshire trying to evict his tenant, having rented it 10 years ago on a 'gentleman's handshake'. Now, however, he is owed a staggering Â£40,000 in unpaid rent.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Their landlord is reluctant to complete the work and has instead served them with an eviction notice. Plus, the case of a Birmingham landlord who wants rid of his troublesome tenants, who have not paid rent for more than a year.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Housing officers in Harrow receive a tip-off about an overcrowded property and when they arrive they find 10 people living in dire conditions and a house that needs extensive emergency work. Squatters in Tottenham invade an elderly woman's home while she is away, ransacking the property and refusing to leave. In Waltham Abbey a seemingly perfect tenant forges her way into a desperate landlord's home, forcing him to move in with his parents while he struggles to evict his unruly occupant.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. Was he able to sell his property at least, or was he tempted to become a landlord once again?(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmor chase over Â£1,500 of unpaid parking fines in Manchester, while Matt and Garry have a job to reclaim Â£1,600 for a car hire company in the West Midlands. Finally, Stewart and Vic head to Lancashire to recover over Â£3,000 owed by a garage to a dissatisfied customer.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, agents Matt and Garry chase Â£1,700 owed by an accountant to a client, and officers Max and Paul try to recover almost Â£30,000 in north west London owed in rent arrears by a dentist after he was barred from working. Meanwhile, agents Max and Steve head to south west London to chase almost Â£2,000 owed by a cafe owner.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry try to recover more than Â£6,000 owed in nursery fees in Warwickshire, while Gary and Cona head to West Sussex, chasing Â£11,000 owed by a businessman who has defaulted on a previous plan, and have no option but to break into the property. The pair also head down to the south coast of England, seeking more than Â£3,500 owed for an unpaid loan.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes Â£2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. But when her mother also gets involved, tempers start to fray. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect Â£5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: An Alzheimer's patient takes part in a clinical trial, but unexpected side-effects trigger an explosive temper and expose a conflict between him and his wife. Meanwhile, House confronts new boss Foreman, and Wilson treats a patient who claims to be in a chaste marriage. Medical drama, starring Hugh Laurie.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The team is forced to perform invasive surgery on a homeless girl, and discovers a complex relationship with her mother when she arrives at her estranged daughter's bedside. Meanwhile, Taub has a difficult time trying to connect with his infant daughters. Medical drama, starring Hugh Laurie and Peter Jacobson.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost Â£8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect Â£3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost Â£2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly Â£3,500 from a businessman in West London.(n)

programme.title: Make You Laugh Out Loud
programme.desc: Compilation of internet videos featuring fathers doing bizarre things, including a showcase of the world's most ridiculous dad dancing and some DIY dentistry using a helicopter to remove a loose tooth. With contributions from comedians Ellie Taylor, Angela Barnes and Dave Spikey.(n)

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Homeless
programme.desc: Showjumper Eleanor, 19, started up her own business as a horse groom, but is now heading onto the streets, believing that the homeless could do with a little geeing up.(n)

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat
programme.desc: Eighteen-year-old William, who is autistic and has eaten the same lunch of burger and chips every day for two years, goes on a tailored diet to reduce his calorie intake by half.(n)

programme.title: The Faculty (1998)
programme.desc: Six high-school students notice their teachers behaving strangely, and realise they have been replaced by shape-changing aliens who are planning to take over the world. Robert Rodriguez's sci-fi horror, starring Elijah Wood, Jordana Brewster, Josh Hartnett, Robert Patrick and Famke Janssen.(n)

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip.(n)

programme.desc: Six high-school students notice their teachers behaving strangely, and realise they have been replaced by shape-changing aliens who are planning to take over the world. Robert Rodriguez's sci-fi horror, starring Elijah Wood, Jordana Brewster, Josh Hartnett, Robert Patrick and Famke Janssen.(n)

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Matter of Honour (1976)
programme.desc: The sleuth's holiday in Mexico is disrupted by a bizarre death involving a suave retired bullfighter, who has killed his best friend using one of the animals as a murder weapon - but is determined his spotless image and reputation will remain untarnished. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ricardo Montalban, A Martinez and Pedro Armendariz Jr.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Requiem for a Falling Star (1973)
programme.desc: A vicious gossip columnist digs the dirt on an ageing film actress, who responds by taking on her most challenging role yet - as a real-life murderer. But when the actress kills her own assistant in error, she looks for a way to shift the blame. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Anne Baxter, Mel Ferrer, Kevin McCarthy and multi-Oscar-winning costume designer Edith Head in a guest appearance as herself.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Blueprint for Murder (1972)
programme.desc: An architect kills his business partner, and comes up with an elaborate scheme to make Columbo first suspect him, then reject him as the murderer. The villain begins by teasing the detective with hints on where he has hidden the body - hoping that when Columbo fails to find a corpse, he will look elsewhere for a killer. Crime drama, directed by and starring Peter Falk. With Patrick O'Neal and Janis Paige.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Old Fashioned Murder (1976)
programme.desc: A woman who has devoted her life to running her family's museum is outraged when her brother decides to sell it. Knowing of someone with criminal connections, she arranges to have her brother killed. However, just when she starts to think she has got away with it, a certain shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Joyce Van Patten, Celeste Holm and Tim O'Connor.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan begins to investigate the murder of a police recruit, but the death of a casual girlfriend leaves him too distracted to concentrate, and he bows out of the case. In his absence, Eames steps in to assist Wheeler - and together they follow the trail of evidence to a surprising suspect. Drama, starring Chris Noth, Julianne Nicholson and Kathryn Erbe.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler are faced with the gruesome task of identifying an animal rights activist found decapitated in a park during a romantic rendezvous. The team try to find two potential killers who were in the vicinity - but are later stunned to discover the crime scene was staged to mislead investigators. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Fin investigate the case of a woman found raped and murdered underneath a flyover - and discover $1million of a new cancer drug in the boot of her car. Analysis reveals the dead woman's supplies were fake, linking her to a pharmaceutical racket - but another assault gives the detectives a more immediate concern. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Ice-T.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man accused of the sexual abuse of his daughter is subsequently murdered, but laying the blame at the door of the chief suspects proves difficult. Several family members are in the frame, but despite evidence of a gun outside the victim's office, they appear to be innocent.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the ritualistic murder of a boy, a woman and an elderly man, and finds they all figured in sexual abuse cases investigated by Benson. As more dead bodies turn up, it becomes clear the detective is the killer's next target - but the others find themselves unable to prove anything against the most likely suspect.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Former Miami detective Jesse Cardoza is drafted in to fill the void left by Delko's absence. However, he is immediately taken hostage by a gunman who has stormed the headquarters to demand that Horatio help him in his quest for justice. Crime drama, starring David Caruso and Eddie Cibrian.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team tries to solve the mystery of how three volleyball players came to die of electrocution while competing in a beach tournament. Meanwhile, as the internal investigation draws to a close, Calleigh's testimony threatens Delko's future. Drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd (Charlie's Angels) and Tia Carrere (Wayne's World).(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits Quebec to testify on behalf of an old friend wrongly accused of murdering his wife. Starring Angela Lansbury, Patrick McGoohan, Juliet Mills and Claire Trevor.(n)

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: an ER physician is gunned down in the street, Castle and Beckett soon discover that the victim was a mob doctor to a Mafia family run by an old friend of the author. Plus, Esposito and Lanie reach a defining moment in their relationship.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green believe the fatal stabbing of psychologist Tracy Conley may be linked to confidential client information on her computer. They learn one of the files on her hard drive has been tampered with since her death - information that would have ensured a known rapist spends another five years behind bars. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green comes under investigation by Internal Affairs after he shoots a suspect, and the case reveals a host of secrets the detective would rather keep hidden, threatening not only his police career but his freedom. Jesse L Martin stars, alongside S Epatha Merkerson and Linus Roache.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine veteran confesses to the murder of a friend 60 years previously, but Gibbs decides to investigate further rather than take his story at face value, hoping to save the elderly Medal of Honour recipient from a spell in prison. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team arrest an ensign in connection with the death of a Navy commander who had recently undergone surgery, but Abby believes they have got the wrong man. Another fatality soon follows - and it falls to Tony to save his colleagues. Starring Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a woman killed in a fall from her art studio rooftop. When the post-mortem reveals the presence of a drug in her system, detectives suspect she was doped - but there is a long list of suspects. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Two thieves discover the skeletal remains of a woman who was believed to have died in the September 11 attacks on the World Trade Centre. It transpires that a valuable and unusual ring has been stolen from the body, and the team suspects foul play. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Elisabeth Rohm and Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are assigned to investigate the execution-style killing of a prominent businessman, and discover his wife was having an affair. However, further evidence emerges, suggesting a different motive - and an unlikely conspirator. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony poses as a convict and takes part in a bogus jailbreak as part of an undercover operation to recover millions of dollars' worth of art and archaeological finds looted from Iraq - but disappears while manacled to a murderous fellow inmate. Guest starring Frank Whaley (Pulp Fiction), with Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A private investigator finds the body of a missing Navy officer in a car at the bottom of a lake and Gibbs reluctantly allows him to assist the investigation into the cause of death. The promise of a $1million reward proves to be a major incentive for the outsider, and the discovery of a bullet leads to suspicions that the man was murdered.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Agents in Washington, DC search for their missing colleagues, while Vance and Torres are summoned to a congressional hearing regarding the fallout from the Paraguay mission.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Dr Huang try to prove a convicted serial killer is responsible for the rape and murder of another woman, whose assailant was never identified. In doing so, they hope to give the victim's parents a sense of closure - but face a race against time as the man is due to be executed in three days. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, BD Wong and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler's wife tells him about a teenage rape victim at the local hospital who is too frightened to go to the police. While trying to find out the girl's identity, he uncovers drug and alcohol abuse at the local school that has him fearing for his daughter's safety - and afraid he may jeopardise his spouse's job. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The kidnapping of a baby at a grocery store leads the detectives to a shady racket that involves snatching infants and arranging to sell them for adoption. Unexpectedly, the investigation produces new leads on an old case involving a murdered mother and her missing child - which had been supervised by Cragen and his partner. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An HIV-positive man with a record of assaulting under-age girls is suspected of a series of rapes and murders - but Stabler's determination to charge him results in tragedy. Undeterred, the team pursues its new suspect, a rap promoter, though his cunning leaves little hope of a conviction. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A crooked investment broker who made many enemies by cheating people out of money is found murdered on board his plane. The investigation turns up no shortage of people who may have wanted to kill him, several of whom had the opportunity - but his hidden stash of funds proves more difficult to locate. Crime drama, starring David Caruso.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Alexx returns to help her former colleagues find the origins of a deadly strain of E.coli. The investigation leads to a large food production corporation, but the unwillingness of its chief executive to co-operate makes life difficult for the team and takes a serious emotional toll on Delko. Crime drama, starring Khandi Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits an old friend, now the Mother Superior of a convent, and finds herself investigating the puzzling death of a nun. Starring Angela Lansbury and Jane Powell.(n)

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Beckett attends the scene of the murder of a pre-school admissions director, and is shocked when Castle turns up brandishing his new credentials as a licensed private investigator. Mystery drama, starring Nathan Fillion.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl is found suffocated to death in Central Park, Briscoe and Green discover her adoptive mother's attempts to discipline the child may have inadvertently caused the tragedy. However, the case is thrown into confusion when a post-mortem reveals she may actually have died of an allergic reaction. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A novelist's apparent suicide becomes a murder case, with the evidence leading Lupo and his new partner Kevin Bernard to suspect a secretive religious group. Award-winning police drama, starring Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A legal-aid strike leads first to murder, and then to Rubirosa pitting her wits against Cutter as the ADA tries to bring a case against a pro golfer accused of the killing of a paralegal. American legal drama, starring Alana De La Garza and Linus Roache.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's wife kills an intruder and later claims he tried to rape her. The team embarks on a mystifying wild goose chase through a stack of old case files and eventually discovers the woman was leading a double life while her husband was serving overseas.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony poses as a convict and takes part in a bogus jailbreak as part of an undercover operation to recover millions of dollars' worth of art and archaeological finds looted from Iraq - but disappears while manacled to a murderous fellow inmate. Guest starring Frank Whaley (Pulp Fiction), with Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A private investigator's murder draws Briscoe and Green toward a case of corruption in a baseball league - where the manager will do anything to ensure his team wins. They learn the victim had unearthed a scandal in which an ineligible player was being fielded in every game - and his discovery may have led to his death. Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green look into the shooting of defence lawyer Vance Grodie, who had just won an acquittal for a suspected cop killer. Their investigations reveal a link to a network of conservative extremists, and the discovery leads the case into an examination of civil rights. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baseball star becomes the prime suspect in the murder of his limousine driver, who had been regularly supplying him with steroids. Briscoe and Green's investigation reveals blackmail as the underlying motive for the crime. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The suspected murder of a petty officer turns out to be a ghoulish practical joke - but a genuine death follows shortly afterwards. As inquiries proceed, local investigators are also assigned to the case - and prove to have personal characteristics remarkably similar to those of Gibbs's staff. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky identifies two dismembered bodies as those of a prosecutor and a sheriff he worked with on a rape case 10 years previously, and realises he could be the killer's next target. Despite Gibbs' attempts to protect him, he is later kidnapped - and the team's search for clues leads to a macabre showdown at a funeral home. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Detective Eames is forced to re-examine the circumstances behind her officer husband's murder when his former partner is killed on the job, and revisits a life that she thought long buried. Crime drama, starring Kathryn Erbe and Vincent D'Onofrio.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate the rape of a cellist, and initially believe her conductor is to blame for the attack - especially given his penchant for erotic film-making. However, they soon discover an obsessive stalker is watching her from afar - and will stop at nothing to continue his monitoring. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Cabot takes a child abuse case, determined to find evidence that will convict the defendant. Her personal feelings tempt her to bend the rules, leading Stabler and Benson into an illegal search of the victim's home - a move that threatens all of their careers.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A urologist is murdered, and his wife claims she was raped and robbed by the killer. Stabler and Benson suspect the doctor's mistress of being involved - until she too is sexually assaulted, and details of another relationship emerge, shedding light on the culprit's identity. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: B&B hosts Kay and Andy are looking to upscale their hospitality business by setting up a larger and grander version on the heritage coast of Snowdonia.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team visits Norfolk to design a bedroom with an en-suite bathroom for Liz and Neil Capper. Following several back operations, Neil had to tackle a tiny staircase on crutches if he wanted to use a bathroom in the night. Once work begins, Julian Perryman frets about his weight and Nick Knowles moans about a tiling job.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Compilation programme centred on a day in Nick Knowles's life, in which the team revisits two of the greatest projects from the series, before finding out how the families involved have fared. Billy Byrne also explores his talents with the English language, and there is a chance to find out what happens behind the scenes.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Loughborough to meet a family of five, whose identical twin babies have been diagnosed as having a form of dwarfism, while at the age of 41, dad Mark is recovering from aggressive bowel and liver cancer. Now they are struggling for space. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their home, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Epsom to meet paramedics Sam and Ben Laws, who have two children, one with special needs. The couple already find it hard, working opposite shifts which mean they often don't see each other for days on end - and to make matters worse, they have found their house to be inadequate for their requirements. After a close friend nominates them for the DIY SOS treatment, Nick and the team - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - turn up to transform it into a home they can enjoy as a family, now and in the future.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning - Day One
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Anita Manning set off on a trip around Hertfordshire and Oxfordshire as they seek out bargains that will earn them a profit. They drive around the scenic areas of both counties, en route to an auction in Banbury.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning - Day Two
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning begin the second leg of their road trip around the south west of England, learning about one of the UK's great inventing families and how a mistake on a map resulted in the naming of America.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning - Day Three
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning begin the third leg of their road trip around the south-west of England, each hoping to make a resounding profit on the items they take to auction.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning - Day Four
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Anita Manning begin their day in south Somerset, before travelling to their auction in Wimborne, Dorset for the fourth leg of their trip. Along the way, Anita learns about the life of prehistoric people at Cheddar Gorge, and Thomas discovers how one pioneering man battled to forecast the weather.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Anita Manning - Day Five
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Anita Manning begin the fifth and final day of their road trip in the seaside town of Brighton, battling it out to take the lead as they search for bargains to sell at auction.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lincolnshire, Middlesex and Cumbria.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore a house in Stoke-on-Trent, a plot of land in west London and a cottage in North Yorkshire. They discover who bought them at auction, before catching up with the buyers when the renovation work has been completed.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore a flat in Cornwall, a house in Co Durham and a property in Chiswick, west London. They then follow each of the sales at auction and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore a one-bedroom flat in Cardiff, a house in Dover, Kent, and an end-of-terrace property in Middlesbrough, all of which have been sold at auction.(n)

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to the Devon seaside town of Torquay to try to transform the fortunes of the Harmony B&B. Dutch owners Hans and Monika have been struggling to attract enough guests through the usually profitable summer months, and are in desperate need of profits now they are faced with the winter low season. Could the couple's love of music-themed decor be putting off potential customers?(n)

programme.title: Taken at Birth
programme.desc: Jane and Lisa meet the first Hicks baby, Stephen Dilbeck. Plus, Chris and Hicks' granddaughter Sallie open a vault in search of birth records that may be inside.(n)

programme.title: Taken at Birth
programme.desc: After 70 years of searching, Stephen receives some shocking news regarding his birth family. Chris and Sallie are close to getting important information.(n)

programme.title: Ghost Adventures: Serial Killer Spirits
programme.desc: Investigating the locations of America's most notorious serial killers. The crew investigates a home in Indiana where HH Holmes killed a young boy.(n)

programme.title: Ghost Loop
programme.desc: The team head to Clear Spring in Maryland, to investigate claims that an unseen entity pushed a woman down the stairs at her historic home.(n)

programme.title: Deadly Possessions
programme.sub-title: Robert the Doll and the Dibbuk Box
programme.desc: Zak Bagans, host of Ghost Adventures, fulfils a lifelong dream of opening a museum in downtown Las Vegas, full of the haunted and cursed objects he has been collecting through the years. Here, Zak comes face to face with a 100-year-old doll who inspired a horror movie franchise. Then, he consults a Las Vegas rabbi after examining a deadly wine cabinet with a connection to the Holocaust.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Girl in the Headlines (1963)
programme.desc: An inspector investigating the murder of a fashion model is led straight into the lair of an international drug-smuggling ring. Crime drama, starring Ian Hendry, Ronald Fraser, Jeremy Brett, Margaret Johnston and Natasha Perry.(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby.(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity (1957)
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley provides tips on curing cramp, while Dr Saleyha Ahsan shows how to spot the signs of a deep vein thrombosis, and Dr Chris van Tulleken reveals the truth about what is actually contained in herbal supplements sold in the UK. Surgeon Gabriel Weston travel to the US to witness the remarkable treatment for a hospital superbug that cures within hours.(n)

However, completing the challenge is made more complicated when one of them drops dead. Comedy, starring Katy Wix and Kenneth Collard.(n)

programme.desc: Observational documentary revealing the inner workings of Oxford Street, following police and paramedics as they keep the public safe, as well as the staff in the shops and on the underground.(n)

programme.title: Warrior Challenge

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Martha's loyalty is tested as she defends Jody Farr, the man responsible for the death of her former client Brendan Kay. However, when she suspects he is not being given a fair trial, she puts her personal feelings to one side and sets a dangerous trap to uncover the truth. Clive focuses all his efforts on the prosecution in a bid to forget his forthcoming tribunal, while Billy faces an uncertain future - no thanks to dodgy solicitor Micky. Maxine Peake, Rupert Penry-Jones and Neil Stuke star.(n)

programme.title: A Perfect Man (2013)
programme.desc: A woman who left her husband after discovering a series of affairs phones him pretending to be another woman, in the hope of resolving her feelings over her broken marriage. The conversation that ensues between them makes him begin to realise what he has lost. Drama, starring Jeanne Tripplehorn and Liev Schreiber.(n)

programme.title: The Flowers of War (2011)
programme.desc: An American drifter arrives in Nanjing in 1937, just as Japanese forces invade. Hiding out in a Western-run church, he poses as a priest, but ends up adopting his role and providing shelter for orphans and courtesans as carnage unfolds in the city. Drama, starring Christian Bale and Ni Ni. In Nanjing dialect, English, Japanese, Mandarin and Shanghainese.(n)

programme.title: I Am Pilate (2020)
programme.desc: Short film about a journalist who is sent to the home of former district governor, only for his routine obituary fact check to reveal an extraordinary secret.(n)

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: A PR coach convinces the girls they will be more popular if they adopt a goth image - but their gloomy new look has the opposite effect.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls shoot a music video on a tropical island, but their presence angers a volcano god, who demands a human sacrifice.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The women of the village revolt against the men when Tjure refuses to help them with their household chores.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby takes over Ylvi's treehouse and will not let her in. Vic comes up with a plan to scare him away, but it goes badly wrong.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra writes a play about a brave animal and a princess.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer causes trouble after getting caught in Zigby's giant kite.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack grows a giant beanstalk, and finds there is a hungry demon at the top of it.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack tries to save the village from a swarm of ravenous butterflies, but ends up accidentally causing a volcanic eruption.(n)
